zh-tw.title: 自愛語錄
zh-tw.description: 你不需要先變得更好，才值得被自己疼愛。把自己排第一，不是自私，是必要。

en.title: Self-Love Quotes
en.description: You don't need to be better first to deserve your own kindness. Putting yourself first isn't selfish — it's necessary.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Cloud-Inc self-love quotes blog; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你不需要讓每一個人都喜歡你。
能夠真心喜歡自己，就已經很了不起了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給太在意別人眼光的朋友，或最近被「合不來的人」消耗的自己。也很適合貼在 IG Stories 當作每週一的開機提醒。
variations: 你不是糖果，本來就不會每個人都愛吃你。

en.content:
You don't need everyone to like you.
Liking yourself, sincerely — that alone is something.
en.editorNote: For a friend who's been worrying too much about how others see them, or for yourself after a draining week. Works as a Monday-morning reset post on Instagram Stories.
variations: You're not candy. Not everyone has to be into you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'talk to yourself like someone you love' affirmation; rephrased

zh-tw.content: 用對最好朋友說話的語氣，對自己說話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 下次又對自己說「我怎麼這麼笨」之前，先問：「我會對朋友這樣講嗎？」答案是不會，那就別這樣對自己。當作每天的提醒語。
variations: 你願意原諒朋友的事情，也願意原諒自己嗎？

en.content: Talk to yourself the way you'd talk to your best friend.
en.editorNote: Next time you catch yourself saying 'I'm so stupid,' ask: would I say that to a friend? No? Then don't say it to yourself. Pin it somewhere you'll see daily.
variations: You forgive your friends. Try forgiving yourself the same way.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 周慕姿 self-care vs selfishness theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
把自己照顧好，不是自私。
是讓你還有力氣去愛別人的前提。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總是把自己排在最後一位的朋友，特別是當媽媽、當主管、長期照顧人的人。提醒他們：你的杯子先裝滿，才有水可以倒給別人。
variations: 你不是補給站，你也需要被補給。

en.content:
Taking care of yourself isn't selfish.
It's what lets you still have something left for the people you love.
en.editorNote: For someone who always puts themselves last — especially moms, managers, caretakers. Remind them: fill your own cup first, or there's nothing left to pour.
variations: You're not a refueling station. You need refueling too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I am enough as I am' affirmation; rephrased with permission framing

zh-tw.content:
我已經夠好了。
就算今天什麼也沒完成。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合躺在床上滑手機、突然開始自責的那種夜晚。提醒自己：你的價值不是用「今天做了什麼」來計算的。
variations: 存在本身就是一種貢獻，你不需要每天都產出什麼。

en.content:
I'm enough as I am.
Even on the days I get nothing done.
en.editorNote: For those nights you're scrolling in bed and suddenly feeling guilty about the day. Reminder: your worth isn't measured in productivity.
variations: Existing is enough. You don't have to output something every day.

items.sourceNote: Combines 接住 (Threads 2026-Q1 trend keyword, 284K mentions) with self-love framing

zh-tw.content: 沒有人接住你的時候，記得自己先接住自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 情緒崩潰但身邊一時找不到人時看的話。重點不是要求自己堅強，而是提醒：你本來就有能力陪自己一段。適合存在手機裡備用。
variations: 你也可以是那個會接住你的人。

en.content: When no one's there to catch you, remember to catch yourself first.
en.editorNote: For the moment your feelings crash and there's no one around. Not asking you to be strong — reminding you that you can still be there for yourself for a while. Save it on your phone.
variations: You can also be the one who catches you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by boundary-setting self-love theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
學會說「不」的那一天，
才是真正開始愛自己的那一天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總是對什麼都說好、答應到自己沒時間的朋友。第一次拒絕別人會不舒服，第十次就習慣了，第一百次你會謝謝當初敢說不的自己。
variations: 拒絕別人不是壞，是把時間留給真正在乎的人。

en.content:
The day you learn to say no
is the day you actually start loving yourself.
en.editorNote: For someone who says yes to everything until they have no time left. The first 'no' feels awful. The hundredth feels like freedom. Send it when a friend is overcommitted.
variations: Saying no isn't mean. It's keeping time for what actually matters.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'be better than yesterday' self-improvement framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你不需要比誰更好。
只需要比昨天的自己，多喜歡自己一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合 IG 限動或週一早上的打氣文。差別在於不催促任何人變強，只要求每天進步一點點對自己的好感度。
variations: 進步不是和別人比，是和昨天的自己比。

en.content:
You don't need to be better than anyone.
Just like yourself a little more than you did yesterday.
en.editorNote: Works for an Instagram Story or a Monday-morning pep talk. The point isn't pushing anyone to be stronger — just asking for a tiny daily uptick in self-fondness.
variations: Progress isn't about beating others. It's about yesterday's you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'solo activities are self-companionship' framing; rephrased for Taiwan context

zh-tw.content:
一個人吃飯，一個人看電影，一個人散步。
這不叫孤單，這叫陪自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給最近因為太多飯局而疲憊的朋友，或剛分手、剛搬到新城市需要重新習慣一個人的人。一個人不是失敗，是練習自己也是好的陪伴。
variations: 獨處不是沒人愛你，是你終於有時間愛自己。

en.content:
Eating alone. Watching a movie alone. Walking alone.
That's not loneliness. That's keeping yourself company.
en.editorNote: For a friend burned out from too many group dinners, or someone freshly single, or anyone in a new city relearning solo life. Being alone isn't failure — it's practicing being your own decent company.
variations: Being alone doesn't mean no one loves you. It means you finally have time to love yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by anti-people-pleasing self-love quotes; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你不是來這個世界讓所有人滿意的。
你是來活成你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 獻給長期當「公司裡好說話的人」、「家族裡懂事的小孩」、「群組裡負責協調的角色」的人。你已經為別人活很久了，可以開始為自己活了。
variations: 讓所有人滿意的人，最後通常都對自己最不滿意。

en.content:
You didn't come here to make everyone happy.
You came here to live as yourself.
en.editorNote: For the office 'easygoing one,' the family 'sensible kid,' the group chat 'peacekeeper.' You've lived for everyone else long enough. You can start living for you.
variations: People who make everyone happy usually end up unhappy with themselves.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by gentle-with-yourself healing tone overlapping with self-love; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
對自己再溫柔一點。
世界已經夠用力了，不差你這一拳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合對自己很苛刻、什麼都要求完美的朋友。或是工作完一整天還在檢討自己的你。已經被世界打了一天，你不用再多踩自己一腳。
variations: 別當自己的差評區，當自己的後援會。

en.content:
Be gentler with yourself.
The world's hitting hard enough — it doesn't need your help.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's hard on themselves and demands perfection. Or for you, after a long day still picking apart what you did. The world's already taken its swings — don't add yours.
variations: Stop being your own bad review section. Be your own fan club.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by intrinsic-worth affirmations ('worth doesn't need to be earned'); rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不需要被別人看見才存在。
它一直都在你身上，沒打過烊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給最近沒得到應有肯定、開始懷疑自己的朋友。沒人按讚不代表你沒價值，沒人稱讚不代表你做得不好。價值本來就不靠別人來認證。
variations: 沒人鼓掌不代表表演不精彩，可能只是觀眾在打瞌睡。

en.content:
Your worth doesn't need anyone's eyes to exist.
It's been there the whole time. Doesn't take days off.
en.editorNote: For someone who's been overlooked lately and starting to doubt themselves. No likes doesn't mean no value. No applause doesn't mean a bad show. Worth doesn't need outside verification.
variations: No applause doesn't mean a bad show. The audience might just be asleep.

items.sourceNote: 2026 is the new 2016 nostalgia trend (Wikipedia entry confirmed); contrast format with self-love angle

zh-tw.content:
2016 的我：等別人喜歡我。
2026 的我：先喜歡自己再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「2016 vs 2026」對照格式正當紅。這版本適合 Threads 短貼，講十年前那個一直求被愛的自己，跟現在那個學會自己疼自己的自己之間的距離。
variations: 2016 的我：發限動等他來看。2026 的我：發限動因為我喜歡這張照片。

en.content:
2016 me: Waited for someone to like me.
2026 me: Decided to like myself first.
en.editorNote: The '2016 vs 2026' contrast format is having a moment. Good for a short Threads post — the gap between who waited to be wanted and who finally decided to start with themselves.
variations: 2016 me: Posted a story hoping he'd see it. 2026 me: Posted a story because I liked the photo.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'unconditional self-love including flaws' theme; rephrased with concrete details

zh-tw.content:
我有缺點，有黑眼圈，有壞習慣。
然後呢？我還是值得被愛——包括被自己愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對著鏡子反省自己每一個瑕疵的那種早晨適合用。自愛不是等變得完美再開始愛，是現在這個版本就值得了。
variations: 等完美再愛自己？那你大概一輩子都等不到。

en.content:
I have flaws. I have dark circles. I have bad habits.
So what? I'm still worthy of love — including my own.
en.editorNote: Save it for the mornings you're cataloging every imperfection in the mirror. Self-love isn't waiting until you're perfect — this version of you, today, already qualifies.
variations: Waiting for perfect to start loving yourself? You'll be waiting forever.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'put yourself first as responsibility, not selfishness' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
把自己排在第一順位，不是自私，是責任。
沒有你，沒有人能照顧你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給因為「自私」這兩個字不敢拒絕、不敢休息、不敢買點小東西犒賞自己的人。照顧自己是你對自己最基本的責任，沒人可以代班。
variations: 你不是 24 小時便利商店，可以打烊。

en.content:
Putting yourself first isn't selfish — it's responsibility.
Without you, no one's left to take care of you.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's afraid of the word 'selfish' so they don't say no, don't rest, don't buy themselves small treats. Caring for yourself is a baseline duty — nobody else can clock in for you.
variations: You're not a 24-hour store. You're allowed to close.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by morning mirror affirmation rituals; rephrased with conversational warmth

zh-tw.content:
早上看著鏡子，記得對裡面那個人說一句：
「今天也辛苦你了，謝謝你還願意陪我。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 起床氣很重、覺得世界都對不起自己的早晨，這句話會把第一個情緒扭回正軌。每天三秒鐘的儀式感，比任何昂貴的保養品都實用。
variations: 刷牙的時候順便對自己笑一下，免費的早安。

en.content:
Look in the mirror in the morning and tell the person inside:
"Thanks for sticking with me one more day."
en.editorNote: On the mornings you wake up annoyed at everything, this resets the first emotion of the day. A three-second ritual that does more than any pricey skincare.
variations: Smile at yourself while you brush. Free good-morning.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'self-love is a daily practice, not a destination' framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是某天突然會的事，
是每天都要練習一點點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種期待「某天終於會愛自己」的人。自愛比較像健身或語言學習，不會某天突然開竅，但每天練一點，半年後會發現自己已經不一樣了。
variations: 今天少罵自己一次，就算那一點。

en.content:
Self-love isn't something you suddenly figure out one day.
It's something you practice, a little at a time.
en.editorNote: For anyone waiting for the day they 'finally love themselves.' Self-love is more like the gym or learning a language — no overnight unlock, but six months of small practice and you're a different person.
variations: One less self-insult today. That counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by viral 2026 Threads post '留一點力氣給自己，而不是全給別人'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
今年要練習的事：
留一點力氣給自己，不要全部給別人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種下班還在回 LINE、放假還在處理同事的事、什麼都答應到自己累趴的人。把自己當作最後一塊吐司，分完別人就沒了。今年至少留兩片給自己。
variations: 別人請你幫忙之前，先問問自己今天的電量還剩多少。

en.content:
This year's practice:
Keep some energy for yourself. Don't give it all away.
en.editorNote: For the person still answering messages after work, fixing a coworker's mess on their day off, saying yes until they're wrecked. You're not the last slice of bread to be handed out — keep a couple for yourself this year.
variations: Before saying yes to a favor, check how much battery you've got left.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 vocus article '改變不是打敗自己，而是陪自己一下'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
成長不是把舊的自己打倒，
是牽著她的手，一起走下一段路。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合給那種一直想「改掉以前的自己」、覺得過去那個我很糟的人。其實她沒有不好，她只是用當時會的方式撐到現在。別罵她，謝謝她。
variations: 以前的你不是要被丟掉的版本，是把你帶到這裡的人。

en.content:
Growing up isn't beating down your old self.
It's taking her hand and walking the next stretch together.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps trying to 'fix' who they used to be and feels embarrassed about past versions. She wasn't bad — she just got you here with the tools she had. Don't shame her. Thank her.
variations: The old you isn't a draft to delete. She's the one who brought you this far.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'choosing self-love is a quiet rebellion' 2026 IG caption trend; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
在大家都在比誰過得更好的時代，
選擇好好愛自己，是一種安靜的反抗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 看完一輪 IG 限動覺得每個人都過得比自己精彩的時候適合用。重點是不必追上誰，能在自己的節奏裡活著，本身就已經是贏。
variations: 你不需要贏過誰，只需要不再輸給昨天的焦慮。

en.content:
In a world where everyone's competing to look better,
choosing to love yourself is a quiet kind of rebellion.
en.editorNote: Use after a doom-scroll through Stories where everyone seems to be living a more glamorous life. You don't have to catch up to anyone — staying in your own rhythm is already the win.
variations: You don't have to outdo anyone. Just stop losing to yesterday's anxiety.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing K-drama quote roundup featuring self-acceptance themes; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
今天可以不堅強。
躺著也是一種愛自己的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我應該振作」但其實還沒準備好的人。允許自己放空一天，不是放棄，是在充電。比逼自己進步重要太多了。
variations: 耍廢也是養分，明天的你會謝謝今天願意休息的你。

en.content:
You don't have to be strong today.
Lying down is also a way of loving yourself.
en.editorNote: For anyone telling themselves 'I should snap out of it' when they're clearly not ready. Letting yourself zone out for a day isn't quitting — it's recharging. Way more important than forcing growth.
variations: Doing nothing counts as nutrition. Tomorrow-you will thank today-you for resting.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 曾寶儀 2026 interview '和解不是終點，而是不斷發生的選擇'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
跟自己和解，不是哪一天突然完成的事。
是每天遇到那個討厭自己的瞬間，再選一次溫柔。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給以為「自愛」是某種終點的人。其實每天都會有那種瞬間想罵自己，重點不是不再有那個瞬間，是在那個瞬間裡願意再給自己一次機會。
variations: 和解不是大事，是小事重複做。

en.content:
Making peace with yourself isn't something you finish one day.
It's choosing kindness again every time you catch yourself hating yourself.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'self-love' has a finish line. Those self-hating moments will keep showing up — the point isn't making them stop, it's deciding to be kind to yourself when they do.
variations: Peace isn't one big move. It's the small one, repeated.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by viral 2026 Threads post '接受自己值得幸福，宇宙才推得進來'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你要先相信自己值得幸福，
好事才知道要往哪裡寄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種一遇到好事就先說「我配嗎」的朋友。不是你不夠好，是你還沒幫宇宙留地址。先點頭說「我值得」，剩下的它會慢慢補上。
variations: 你不開門，再多禮物也只能堆在門口。

en.content:
Believe you deserve good things first.
Otherwise the good things don't know where to send themselves.
en.editorNote: For the friend who reflexively says 'do I even deserve this?' when something nice happens. It's not that you're not enough — you just haven't given the universe an address yet. Nod first, the rest will follow.
variations: If you don't open the door, the gifts just pile up outside.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'look at mistakes with compassion' 2026 self-compassion affirmation; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
犯錯不等於你是壞的版本。
那只代表你今天還在學。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合搞砸一件事之後狂自責的夜晚。錯誤不是判決書，是練習題。你不是壞了，你只是今天的版本還在 beta 測試中。
variations: 把錯誤當教材，不是把自己當垃圾。

en.content:
Making a mistake doesn't mean you're a broken version of yourself.
It just means today you were still learning.
en.editorNote: Save it for the nights you're spiraling after messing something up. A mistake isn't a verdict — it's a practice problem. You're not broken; today's version of you is just still in beta.
variations: Mistakes are study material. You are not garbage.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 manifestation affirmations '不急著證明什麼' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
今年不急著證明什麼。
慢慢長的東西，根才會深。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給看到同學升遷、朋友結婚、誰又買房的時候開始焦慮的你。別人開花的時候你在長根，看不見不代表沒在動。今年留給自己的節奏。
variations: 不是落後，是你的時區還沒到春天。

en.content:
No rush to prove anything this year.
The things that grow slowly are the ones that root deepest.
en.editorNote: For when classmates get promoted, friends get married, and someone bought another house. While others are blooming, you might be growing roots — invisible doesn't mean idle. This year's pace belongs to you.
variations: You're not behind. Your time zone just hasn't hit spring yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing quote '哭不是脆弱，是堅強太久'; rephrased with permission framing

zh-tw.content:
想哭就哭吧。
眼淚不是失敗的證據，是你還會心疼自己的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我都這麼大了還哭什麼」的朋友。哭不丟臉，麻木才是。能為自己掉一滴眼淚，代表你心裡那個人還活著、還有感覺。
variations: 眼淚是身體幫你做的情緒大掃除。

en.content:
Cry if you need to.
Tears aren't proof you failed — they're proof you can still feel something for yourself.
en.editorNote: For the friend who says 'I'm too old to cry over this.' Crying isn't shameful — going numb is. A tear for yourself means the person inside you is still alive, still capable of feeling.
variations: Tears are your body doing emotional housekeeping.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'release self-doubt and embrace self-love' affirmation; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
把那個一直懷疑你的聲音請出去。
那不是你，那只是你住太久的房客。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合每次開始懷疑自己「是不是配不上」、「是不是不夠好」的時候用。那個聲音不是你的真心話，是別人留在你腦中的雜音。請它離開，房子是你的。
variations: 腦中那個壞話最多的人，根本沒交房租。

en.content:
Evict the voice that keeps doubting you.
It's not you. It's a tenant who overstayed.
en.editorNote: For the moments you start questioning whether you 'really deserve it' or 'measure up.' That voice isn't your truth — it's noise other people left in your head. Show it the door. The house is yours.
variations: The harshest voice in your head never even paid rent.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'enjoy yourself without guilt' manifestation affirmation; rephrased with Taiwanese conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
對自己好不需要先賺到。
買杯好咖啡、躺著耍廢一個下午，不用先交報告才能享受。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總覺得「等我做完這個再休息」、「等我瘦下來再買新衣服」的人。獎勵不是發給及格的人，是給願意對自己好的人。今天就可以開始。
variations: 誰規定享受要先繳辛苦稅？

en.content:
You don't have to earn being good to yourself.
A nice coffee, an afternoon doing nothing — no report due first.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps saying 'I'll rest after I finish this' or 'I'll buy new clothes after I lose weight.' Rewards aren't a passing grade. They're for people willing to be kind to themselves. Today qualifies.
variations: Who said enjoyment requires a hard-work tax first?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 viral daily message format around morning self-affirmation; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
今天的功課只有一件：
至少對自己說一句好話。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給連早安都嫌煩的星期一。一句就好，不用很長，「你今天頭髮不錯」也算。每天累積一句，一年就 365 句別人聽不到、但你自己會記得的好話。
variations: 別人不誇你，你就自己誇，免費的。

en.content:
Today's only assignment:
Say at least one nice thing to yourself.
en.editorNote: For the Mondays where even 'good morning' feels like too much. One line is fine — 'your hair looks decent today' counts. One a day adds up to 365 nice things no one else heard, but you'll remember.
variations: If no one's complimenting you, do it yourself. It's free.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 optimistic-start quote roundup '為自己鼓掌' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
別人沒看見你的努力沒關係，
你自己有看見就好。
你才是最該為自己鼓掌的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種默默撐了好久、卻沒人說一句辛苦了的朋友。不是每件事都會有掌聲，但你心裡要有那個會替自己拍手的角落。沒人鼓掌的時候，你自己先鼓。
variations: 你的努力不是表演，不需要每個人都買票。

en.content:
It's okay if no one else sees how hard you tried.
You saw it. That's the part that counts.
You're the one who should be clapping for you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's been holding it together quietly with no one saying 'good job.' Not every effort gets applause — keep a corner inside you that claps for itself. When no one else is, you start.
variations: Your effort isn't a show. Not everyone needs a ticket.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are already a masterpiece, you cannot be improved' Osho-style quote in 2026 update; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
你不是還沒完工的作品，
你只是還沒看清楚自己已經是誰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直覺得「等我再瘦一點、再會講話一點、再賺多一點」才值得喜歡自己的人。你不是半成品，你只是還沒換上能看見自己的眼睛。
variations: 你不是 beta 版，你是正式上線很久了。

en.content:
You're not an unfinished project.
You just haven't looked closely enough to see who you already are.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps saying 'once I'm thinner / better at talking / earning more, then I'll like myself.' You're not a half-built thing — you just haven't put on the glasses that see what's there.
variations: You're not in beta. You went live a long time ago.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your existence is warm sunlight' Marie Claire 2026 healing roundup; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你存在的本身，就是某個人今天最溫暖的事。
當然，那個人也可以先是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得「我又沒做什麼，怎麼配被喜歡」的朋友。光是你願意起床、願意回訊息、願意活著，這件事本身就有溫度。先讓自己感受得到。
variations: 你不需要做出什麼，光是還在這裡，就值得了。

en.content:
Your existing is the warmest thing in someone's day.
That someone can start with you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who says 'I haven't done anything — how am I worth liking?' Just getting up, replying to a message, staying alive — that already gives off warmth. Let yourself feel it first.
variations: You don't have to produce anything. Still being here is enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the world isn't watching you all day, everyone's busy with their own life' Hospital Playlist healing line; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
別人沒有你想像中那麼在意你。
所以也請你，不要那麼苛刻地在意自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種出門前會反覆檢查穿搭、講錯一句話會自責一整晚的人。大家都忙著過自己的人生，沒空一直盯著你。你可以放鬆，沒人在閱卷。
variations: 你以為的全世界看著你，其實只有你在看你自己。

en.content:
People aren't watching you as closely as you think.
So please, stop watching yourself so harshly either.
en.editorNote: For anyone who triple-checks their outfit before leaving the house and replays one awkward sentence all night. Everyone's busy with their own life — nobody's grading you. You can relax.
variations: The 'whole world watching you'? Mostly just you watching yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 motivational quote roundup 'boundaries are courage to love yourself' Brené Brown framing; rephrased with conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
畫界線會讓某些人失望，
但不畫界線，失望的會是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「不想讓別人不開心」所以一直答應、一直忍耐的朋友。讓別人失望一次，比讓自己失望一輩子划算多了。先選自己一次看看。
variations: 界線不是牆，是讓你還能呼吸的那扇窗。

en.content:
Drawing a line will disappoint some people.
Not drawing one will disappoint you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who says yes and keeps tolerating because they 'don't want anyone upset.' Disappointing someone once is a way better deal than disappointing yourself for years. Try picking yourself once.
variations: A boundary isn't a wall. It's the window that lets you breathe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by May 2026 self-love roundup 'self-love is the foundation, without it other love fails' bell hooks framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你無法把自己沒有的東西給出去。
沒有先愛自己，連愛別人都會少一塊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我先把別人照顧好，再來照顧自己」的人。你給出去的愛，大部分是從你怎麼對自己學來的。先把自己那杯倒滿，才能真的分給別人。
variations: 對自己刻薄的人，給別人的溫柔通常也撐不久。

en.content:
You can't hand out what you don't have inside.
Without loving yourself first, the love you give will always be missing a piece.
en.editorNote: For people who say 'I'll take care of others first, myself later.' The love you offer is mostly copied from how you treat yourself. Fill your own cup first, or what you pour out won't last.
variations: Someone harsh with themselves usually can't keep being soft with others for long.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing affirmation 'I choose myself every single day' framing; rephrased with concrete daily-choice angle

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是大事。
是每天的小選擇——多睡那十分鐘、不回那則消耗你的訊息、買那杯你想喝的拿鐵。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得「愛自己」聽起來太抽象、不知道從哪開始的人。其實它從你今天決定的那些不起眼的小事開始。每一個小選擇，都是在告訴自己：我重要。
variations: 今天有多愛自己，看你做的小決定就知道。
別等大事再愛自己，今天那杯拿鐵就夠了。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't a big thing.
It's the small daily picks — those extra ten minutes of sleep, ignoring that draining text, buying the latte you actually wanted.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'self-love' sounds too abstract to start. It actually starts in the small choices you make today. Each tiny pick tells yourself: I matter.
variations: Want to know how much you love yourself today? Look at your small decisions.
Don't wait for big moments. Today's latte counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG caption trend 'powerful when you stop needing approval'; rephrased with conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你開始變強的那一刻，
是你不再需要每個人都點頭的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總是發訊息前要修五次、PO 限動前要先猜大家會不會喜歡的人。你的人生不是民意調查，不需要過半數同意才能繼續。喜歡自己這一票，先投給自己。
variations: 別人按不按讚是他的事，你發不發是你的事。
你不是候選人，幹嘛一直拉票？

en.content:
The moment you start getting strong
is the moment you stop needing everyone to nod.
en.editorNote: For the person who edits a message five times before sending and previews how a Story will land before posting. Your life isn't a popularity poll — you don't need majority approval to continue. Cast your own vote first.
variations: Whether they like it is their business. Whether you post it is yours.
You're not a candidate. Stop campaigning.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 mirror work practice 'inner critic vs higher self' theme; rephrased with conversational reframing

zh-tw.content:
腦中那個一直挑你毛病的聲音，不是真理，是習慣。
習慣可以換，從今天開始換一個對自己說話的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給每次照鏡子第一個念頭就是「臉好腫」、「眼袋好深」的人。那不是事實，是你已經練習太多次的負面自我對話。練習可以改寫，今天從說一句好話開始。
variations: 你不是真的很糟，是你罵自己罵太順了。
腦中的差評不是真相，是劇本。換稿。

en.content:
The voice in your head picking you apart isn't truth — it's habit.
Habits can be swapped. Start with a new way of talking to yourself today.
en.editorNote: For the person whose first thought in the mirror is 'my face looks puffy' or 'these dark circles.' That's not fact — it's a negative self-talk loop you've rehearsed too well. Practice can rewrite it. Start with one kind line.
variations: You're not actually that bad. You've just gotten really fluent at putting yourself down.
The bad reviews in your head aren't truth. They're a script. Rewrite it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing roundup 'true strength is allowing things to happen, not resisting' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
真正的堅強不是什麼都扛得住，
是允許自己有今天扛不住的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直撐著、連在朋友面前都不敢示弱的人。真正的強，不是永遠不倒，是倒下的時候願意承認、願意被接住、願意休息。允許脆弱，才有空間恢復。
variations: 你不是機器人，今天當機沒關係。
硬撐到最後常常不是堅強，是逞強。

en.content:
Real strength isn't carrying everything.
It's letting yourself have a day when you can't.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps pushing through and won't show weakness even to friends. Real strength isn't never falling — it's admitting it when you do, letting yourself be caught, letting yourself rest. Allow the cracks. That's where the recovery fits.
variations: You're not a machine. A crash day is fine.
Pushing through to the end isn't always strength. Sometimes it's just stubbornness.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by viral 2026 Threads line '願你給出去的柔軟，都能以更好的形狀回到你身邊'; rephrased with concrete reciprocity framing

zh-tw.content:
你對自己越溫柔，
世界回給你的版本就越柔軟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「對別人很好，對自己很兇」的朋友。你怎麼對自己，宇宙就怎麼學會對你。先把那份溫柔留一份在自己身上，世界會慢慢跟上。
variations: 你怎麼對自己，就是在教別人怎麼對你。

en.content:
The softer you are with yourself,
the softer the version of the world that comes back to you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's gentle with everyone but rough on themselves. The universe learns how to treat you from how you treat yourself. Keep some of that softness for you — the world will follow.
variations: How you treat yourself is how you teach everyone else to treat you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff self-compassion + 阿德勒 acceptance-of-imperfection themes; rephrased into a single conversational line

zh-tw.content:
你不是來變成完美的版本，
你是來練習怎麼跟不完美的自己當朋友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直想「修好」自己缺點的朋友。自愛不是把所有不順眼的地方磨平，是學會跟那些地方握個手、一起過日子。朋友本來就不會要求對方完美。
variations: 你跟自己是合租人，不是工程驗收員。

en.content:
You're not here to become the perfect version.
You're here to practice being friends with the imperfect one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps trying to 'fix' every flaw. Self-love isn't sanding down everything you don't like — it's learning to shake hands with those parts and live alongside them. Friends don't demand perfection.
variations: You and yourself are roommates, not building inspectors.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I am in control of my happiness' framing; rephrased with ownership angle

zh-tw.content:
你的快樂不需要批准。
沒人比你更有資格決定，你今天要開心什麼事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種開心之前要先看別人臉色、買個小東西也要先想「會不會被笑」的人。快樂的決定權在你手上，不在群組裡的誰、也不在限動底下的留言。
variations: 別人覺得很無聊的事，你開心就是值得。

en.content:
Your happiness doesn't need approval.
Nobody's more qualified than you to decide what counts as a good day.
en.editorNote: For anyone who checks other people's faces before being happy, or worries 'will I get teased?' before buying something small. The decision-making power is yours — not the group chat's, not the Story comments'.
variations: If they think it's silly but you're happy, it still counts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by QuoteFancy 2026 update 'be your own first listener' framing; rephrased with concrete daily-life imagery

zh-tw.content:
在跟別人吐苦水之前，
先停三秒鐘，聽聽自己想說什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給總是急著把情緒倒給別人、卻從沒問過自己「我到底怎麼了」的人。你是自己第一個讀者，先把心裡那段話讀給自己聽，再決定要不要分享給別人。
variations: 情緒不是垃圾，不用急著倒掉。

en.content:
Before you vent to anyone else,
pause three seconds and hear what you actually wanted to say.
en.editorNote: For people who rush to dump feelings onto others without ever asking themselves 'what's actually going on?' You're your own first reader — let yourself hear it before deciding to share.
variations: Feelings aren't trash. No rush to take them out.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 positive-start roundup 'be the person who shows up for yourself' framing; rephrased with appointment metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你跟自己約好的事，也算約。
別人臨時取消你會難過，那你別老是讓自己被放鴿子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「答應自己要早睡」、「答應自己要去運動」結果一直跳票的人。你對自己說的話也是承諾，別人爽約你會生氣，你也要對自己一樣認真。
variations: 別把自己排在所有人之後，你也是名單上的客戶。
你都不赴自己的約，怎麼怪別人不重視你？

en.content:
A plan you made with yourself still counts as a plan.
You'd be hurt if someone bailed last minute — stop doing it to yourself.
en.editorNote: For anyone who keeps promising 'I'll sleep early' or 'I'll go to the gym' and breaking it. What you say to yourself is a commitment too. You'd be upset if a friend ditched — take yourself just as seriously.
variations: Don't put yourself last on the list. You're a client too.
If you keep standing yourself up, why blame others for not showing up?

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Marie Claire 2026 healing roundup 'cracks let the light in' theme; rephrased with conversational reframing

zh-tw.content:
你身上那些裂痕，不是壞掉的證據。
那是光終於可以照進來的縫隙。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得自己「破破爛爛」、不敢讓別人看見傷口的朋友。裂痕不是丟臉，是你撐過了某些事的痕跡。光不會從完美無瑕的地方進來，它需要那道縫。
variations: 傷疤是你的勳章，不是你的把柄。
你不是壞掉，你是經歷過。

en.content:
The cracks in you aren't proof you're broken.
They're where the light finally gets in.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels 'damaged' and hides their wounds. Cracks aren't shameful — they're evidence you got through something. Light doesn't enter through what's flawless. It needs the opening.
variations: Scars are your medals, not your liabilities.
You're not broken. You've been through things.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by QuoteFancy 2026 update 'inner peace is the moment you stop letting others control your emotions' Pema Chödrön framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content: 內在的平靜，從你決定不再讓某個人左右你的心情那一刻開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種今天他已讀不回就毀掉一整天、群組沒人理就開始懷疑人生的朋友。把自己的心情遙控器拿回來，那本來就是你的東西，不該交給別人保管。
variations: 別把你今天開不開心的決定權，外包給一個不夠在乎你的人。
你的情緒不是訂閱制，沒人有權自動扣款。

en.content: Inner peace starts the moment you stop letting one person decide your mood.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose whole day collapses because someone left them on read, or who spirals when the group chat goes quiet. Take back the remote — it was always yours, not theirs to hold.
variations: Don't outsource your daily mood to someone who isn't even invested.
Your emotions aren't on auto-pay. No one gets to charge them without permission.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Brit+Co affirmation 'I am worthy of feeling at peace and deserve to enjoy myself without guilt'; rephrased with anti-guilt framing

zh-tw.content:
你值得開心，不需要先愧疚一下才能享受。
沒人規定快樂前要交報名費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種放假在家耍廢一下午、然後晚上開始自責「我是不是太爽了」的人。你休息的時候世界不會崩塌，你開心的時候也沒有對不起誰。請領取你的快樂，免收手續費。
variations: 享受不用配愧疚當沾醬。
你可以開心，這件事不用先過審。

en.content:
You're allowed to be happy without a side of guilt.
Nobody charges an entry fee for enjoying yourself.
en.editorNote: For the person who loafs around all afternoon, then spends the evening guilty for 'enjoying it too much.' The world doesn't fall apart when you rest. Nobody loses when you're happy. Collect your joy — no processing fee.
variations: Enjoyment doesn't need guilt as a dipping sauce.
You're allowed to be happy. It doesn't need pre-approval.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'self-love is a daily relationship, the longest one you'll have'; rephrased with relationship framing

zh-tw.content:
你和自己這段關係，是你這輩子最久的一段。
別用對短期客人的態度，對待住一輩子的室友。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友很好、對情人也很好，但對自己卻像對陌生人的人。沒人會比你更常出現在你生命裡，這段關係值得你投入。從今天起，把自己當成最重要的長期合作夥伴。
variations: 你跟自己每天都要見面，至少先打個招呼吧。
別人會來會走，只有你會陪自己走完全程。

en.content:
Your relationship with yourself is the longest one you'll ever have.
Stop treating the lifelong roommate like a weekend guest.
en.editorNote: For the person who's warm to friends, warm to partners, and a stranger to themselves. No one shows up in your life as often as you do — invest in this one. Start treating yourself like your most important long-term partner.
variations: You see yourself every single day. At least say hi.
Others come and go. You're the one walking the whole road with you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG trend 'embracing the masterpiece I am, flaws and all'; rephrased with Taiwan conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
你的瑕疵不是要等被修好才能被喜歡，
它們也是你這個人的一部分，一起被愛就好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總在挑自己毛病、覺得「等我改掉這個再說」的朋友。完美不是被愛的門票，你連帶那些不完美，本來就值得整套被收下。
variations: 你不是商品，不用先過品管才能上架。
別把自己當廢稿，那不是真的你。

en.content:
Your flaws aren't waiting to be fixed before you're worth loving.
They're part of you too. Love the whole set.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps picking at themselves saying 'once I fix this, then.' Perfection isn't the ticket to being loved — you, flaws included, were always meant to be taken as a package.
variations: You're not a product. You don't need QA before going on the shelf.
Stop treating yourself like a rough draft. That's not really you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'taking up space unapologetically' caption trend; rephrased with conversational anti-shrink framing

zh-tw.content:
別再把自己縮小，方便別人坐得舒服。
你本來就有資格佔你那塊空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種講話會自動壓低音量、走路會自動靠邊、發限動會自動先想「會不會吵到誰」的人。你的存在不是借來的，不需要每天還利息。坐挺一點，那位子是你的。
variations: 你不是備胎座位，是正駕駛。
別人嫌你太亮，是他眼睛弱，不是你太閃。

en.content:
Stop shrinking yourself so other people can sit comfortably.
You were always entitled to the space you take up.
en.editorNote: For the person who auto-lowers their voice, walks closer to the wall, and pre-worries 'will this bother anyone?' before posting a Story. Your existence isn't borrowed — no interest payments due. Sit up. The seat's yours.
variations: You're not the spare seat. You're in the driver's seat.
If they think you're too bright, that's their weak eyes — not your shine.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 viral Threads roundup 'put your heart in a comfortable position' framing; rephrased with relocation metaphor

zh-tw.content:
今年練習的事：
把心放在舒服的位置，不放在勉強的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種還在勉強自己參加不想去的飯局、回不想回的訊息、待在讓你不舒服的關係裡的人。心也會痠，也需要換姿勢。今年准許自己挑一個自己坐得住的角落。
variations: 勉強住的房子叫違建，勉強過的日子也是。
心要放對位置，不是塞進別人的行程表裡。

en.content:
This year's practice:
Put your heart somewhere it can rest — not somewhere it has to brace.
en.editorNote: For anyone still forcing themselves into dinners they don't want, replies they don't owe, relationships that ache to sit in. Hearts get sore too. They need to shift. This year, let yourself pick a corner you can actually settle into.
variations: A house you live in under duress is an illegal build. Same goes for a life.
Your heart needs the right spot — not someone else's schedule.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG self-love roundup 'I am my own home' framing; rephrased with Taiwan conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
你不需要在別人身上找一個家。
你自己就是那個可以隨身帶著走的家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總是把安全感寄放在某個人身上、對方一冷淡就崩潰的朋友。家不是某個人，是你跟自己相處時感受到的那份穩。你走到哪，那個家就在哪。
variations: 別人不是港口，是船，會走的。你自己才是岸。
你不是無家可歸，你只是還沒發現家就在身上。

en.content:
You don't have to find a home in someone else.
You're the kind of home you carry with you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps storing their safety in one person and crumbles the moment that person goes cold. Home isn't a person — it's the steadiness you feel with yourself. Wherever you go, that home goes with you.
variations: Other people are boats, not harbors. They move. You're the shore.
You're not homeless. You just haven't noticed the home you're carrying.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'flaws? I call them features' viral IG short caption; rephrased with Taiwan tech metaphor

zh-tw.content:
那些你以為是缺點的，
其實是出廠就內建的特色。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給每天照鏡子都在挑自己毛病的朋友。你不是瑕疵品，你只是用錯說明書了。換個角度看，那些被你嫌棄的點，可能正是別人喜歡你的地方。
variations: 瑕疵跟特色，差在誰在解釋。
你不是壞掉，你只是規格跟別人不一樣。

en.content:
The parts you think are flaws
are features that came pre-installed.
en.editorNote: For the friend who picks themselves apart in the mirror every morning. You're not defective — you've just been reading the wrong manual. Reframe it: the things you can't stand might be exactly why someone else loves you.
variations: Flaw or feature — depends who's describing it.
You're not broken. You just shipped with different specs.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 心靈勵志 '允許自己慢慢前行，不必追趕世界的節奏' theme; rephrased with traffic/pace metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你不需要追上任何人的時間表。
人生不是賽道，是你自己的散步路線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種看到同學買房、同事升遷、朋友結婚就開始焦慮的人。每個人的時區不一樣，你準時抵達的，是你自己的人生，不是別人的。今天慢一點也沒關係。
variations: 別人在跑，你在走，你們去的地方根本不同。
焦慮通常是因為你在拿別人的時鐘看自己的時間。

en.content:
You don't need to catch up to anyone's timeline.
Life isn't a track. It's your own walk.
en.editorNote: For the person who spirals every time a classmate buys a house, a coworker gets promoted, or a friend gets married. Everyone's on their own clock — you're on time for your life, not theirs. Going slower today is fine.
variations: They're running, you're walking. You're not even headed to the same place.
Most anxiety is just reading your time on someone else's clock.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG caption 'unbothered, unavailable, unstoppable' viral trend; rephrased with Taiwan conversational reframing

zh-tw.content:
今年新習慣：
別人怎麼看你，他自己想就好，不用你幫忙煩惱。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種會幫別人腦補「他是不是討厭我」、「她是不是覺得我很煩」的人。別人腦袋裡的劇本，從來都不需要你校稿。把那份心力收回來，留給自己。
variations: 別人怎麼想你，是他的家事，不歸你管。
幫人腦補不會讓你被喜歡，只會讓你很累。

en.content:
New habit this year:
What people think of you is their job to think — not yours to worry about.
en.editorNote: For the person who mentally writes other people's scripts — 'do they hate me?' 'do they think I'm annoying?' Their thoughts don't need your editing. Take that energy back and spend it on yourself.
variations: What they think of you is their household business. Not yours to manage.
Doing someone's thinking for them won't get you liked. It'll just exhaust you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'you don't have to earn rest / worth' healing affirmation; rephrased with everyday Taiwanese phrasing

zh-tw.content:
休息不是你做完什麼才能換到的獎品。
是你本來就有的權利。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「沒做完事就不配躺著」的人。你不是付費會員，你的休息不需要儲值才能解鎖。今天累了就先停下，不用先繳成績單。
variations: 你不用先變有用，才能被允許喘一口氣。
罪惡感不會把你充飽電，休息會。

en.content:
Rest isn't a prize you win after finishing things.
It's something you already have a right to.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels they don't deserve to lie down until the to-do list is done. You're not on a paid plan. You don't need to top up your usefulness to unlock rest. Tired today? Stop. No receipts required.
variations: You don't have to be useful first to be allowed to breathe.
Guilt won't recharge you. Rest will.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Threads viral '接受自己值得幸福，宇宙才推得進來' new-year quote; rephrased with door-opening metaphor

zh-tw.content:
好事一直在外面等你，
是你一直沒把那扇「我值得」的門打開。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「機會來了會先說我不行」、「被誇獎會立刻否認」的人。你不是沒運氣，是你習慣性把幸福擋在門外。今天試著回一句「謝謝，我收下了」。
variations: 說「我不值得」說太多次，幸福會以為走錯地址。
宇宙不缺要給你的東西，缺的是你願意接的手。

en.content:
Good things have been waiting outside the whole time.
You just never opened the door labeled 'I deserve this.'
en.editorNote: For the person who says 'I'm not ready' when opportunity knocks, or instantly deflects every compliment. You're not unlucky — you've been keeping good things out by habit. Today, try saying 'thank you, I'll take it.'
variations: Say 'I don't deserve it' enough times and good things start thinking they got the wrong address.
The universe isn't short on gifts for you. It's waiting on your open hands.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff-style self-compassion themes and Brene Brown worthiness framing; rephrased with mirror reframing

zh-tw.content:
鏡子裡那個人，今天也是盡力了。
別忘了跟他說一聲謝謝。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合刷牙、洗臉、睡前那一刻。我們對外送員、對店員都會說謝謝，卻很少對自己說。今晚試一次，看著鏡子裡的自己說：「今天辛苦了，謝謝你撐到現在。」
variations: 你最常忽略的那個人，就住在你的鏡子裡。
對自己說聲謝謝，比按 100 次愛心都還補。

en.content:
The person in the mirror tried their best today too.
Don't forget to say thanks.
en.editorNote: For the moment you're brushing your teeth or about to sleep. We thank the delivery driver, the barista — rarely ourselves. Tonight, look at the mirror and say it out loud: 'Today was hard. Thanks for making it through.'
variations: The person you ignore the most lives in your mirror.
Thanking yourself out loud beats 100 self-care reels.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 vocus self-growth article on accepting current state without self-shame; rephrased with weather metaphor

zh-tw.content:
今天的你不好，沒關係。
天氣都會壞，憑什麼要求自己每天都晴天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合心情突然往下掉、又開始責怪自己「怎麼又這樣」的時刻。情緒跟天氣一樣會變，下雨不是你的錯，也不需要道歉。撐傘等它過就好。
variations: 你不是壞掉，你只是今天陰天而已。
別跟自己的低潮拼輸贏，陪它走完就好。

en.content:
Bad day today? That's fine.
Even the weather has bad days. Why should you be sunny every single one?
en.editorNote: For when your mood suddenly drops and you start scolding yourself for 'doing this again.' Feelings move like weather. Rain isn't your fault and doesn't need an apology. Grab an umbrella and wait it out.
variations: You're not broken. You're just overcast today.
Don't fight your low days. Walk through them with yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'love yourself first' Girlsmood self-love roundup; rephrased with anti-people-pleasing edge

zh-tw.content:
你不是來這世界當大家的便利貼的。
貼太多次，自己就被撕爛了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種別人一開口就點頭、被拜託就不會說不的人。每答應一次不想答應的事，你就被撕下一塊。今天練習一次：「不好意思，這次我沒辦法。」就這樣，後面不用再加解釋。
variations: 你不欠任何人一個「好」。
說「不」不是冷漠，是把自己的時間留給自己。

en.content:
You weren't put here to be everyone's sticky note.
Get used too many times and you tear.
en.editorNote: For the chronic yes-sayer who can't decline a favor. Every yes you didn't mean tears off another piece of you. Practice this today: 'Sorry, I can't this time.' Full stop. No explanation needed after.
variations: You don't owe anyone a yes.
'No' isn't cold. It's keeping your time for yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff's research on self-compassion vs self-esteem; rephrased with everyday framing

zh-tw.content:
你的價值，不會因為今天搞砸了就被扣分。
那是別人發的成績單，不是你本來就有的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種剛把一件事搞砸、然後整個人覺得自己廢掉的下午。提醒一下：你不是「成功才有價值」，你是「本來就有價值，只是今天剛好沒成功」。這兩個是不一樣的。
variations: 成績會變，你不會變。
把「我搞砸了」跟「我是個爛人」這兩件事分開。

en.content:
Your worth doesn't get docked points because today went sideways.
That's someone else's grading sheet — not the worth you came with.
en.editorNote: For the afternoon you just blew something and now feel like a write-off. Reminder: you're not 'worth something because you succeed,' you're 'worth something who happens to not have succeeded today.' Those are two different sentences.
variations: Grades change. You don't.
Separate 'I messed up' from 'I'm a mess.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'stop shrinking yourself to fit in' theme on Minds Journal; rephrased with body-language metaphor

zh-tw.content:
為了塞進別人的位置，你已經縮太久了。
站直一點，那個位置本來就不是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給長期在一段關係、一個職場、一個家庭裡把自己縮小的人。你不是太大，是那個空間太小。試著今天先伸個懶腰，把肩膀打開，看世界會不會塌下來。不會的。
variations: 你縮再小，他們也不會多愛你一點。
合不下的不是你，是那個位置太窄。

en.content:
You've been shrinking too long to fit somewhere that wasn't yours.
Stand up straight. That spot was never your size.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's spent years making themselves smaller in a relationship, job, or family. You're not too much — the space is too narrow. Try this today: stretch, roll your shoulders back, and see if the world collapses. It won't.
variations: Shrinking won't make them love you more.
You don't fit because the space is wrong — not because you are.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Ella Chen's 'don't stop your steps for anyone' quote on Own; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
別人走得快，是他的事。
你的步調，不需要跟誰對齊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合最近一直被「同學都結婚了」、「同事都升遷了」這種比較感壓得喘不過氣的時候。人生不是接力賽，沒有人在計時。你慢慢走，也會到。
variations: 你不是慢，你是走自己的路。
腳步不一樣，不代表方向錯了。

en.content:
Other people walk fast — that's their thing.
Your pace doesn't have to match anyone's.
en.editorNote: For when 'all my classmates are married' or 'all my coworkers got promoted' is suffocating you. Life isn't a relay race. No one's holding a stopwatch. You'll get there walking, too.
variations: You're not slow. You're on your own road.
Different pace doesn't mean wrong direction.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Anna Taylor's 'love yourself enough to set boundaries' theme on Mindfulness Exercises; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
願意拒絕的人，才知道自己的時間值多少。
劃下界線，是愛自己最安靜的方式。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「拒絕別人會有罪惡感」的好人。其實會生氣的，通常都是那些早就習慣占你便宜的人。試著這週說一次「不好意思，這次不行」，不用解釋，看世界會不會塌。不會的。
variations: 說「不」不是壞，是把「好」留給真正重要的人。
你的時間不是吃到飽自助餐。

en.content:
People who can say no are the ones who know what their time is worth.
Drawing a line is the quietest way of loving yourself.
en.editorNote: For the chronic 'yes' person who feels guilty saying no. The people who get upset are usually the ones who were used to taking from you. Try one 'sorry, can't this time' this week — no explanation needed — and see if the world ends. It won't.
variations: Saying no isn't mean. It saves your yes for what matters.
Your time isn't an all-you-can-eat buffet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Maya Angelou's 'you alone are enough, you have nothing to prove' theme; rephrased with a quieter framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用再多做什麼，才值得被自己喜歡。
你已經是夠了，只是還不敢相信而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「等我瘦下來」、「等我升職」、「等我存到一百萬」才肯放過自己的人。那個「等」會等很久，也許永遠不會到。今天就先試著喜歡現在的自己一次，當作一個小實驗。
variations: 你不是不夠，是太習慣用別人的尺量自己。
「我夠了」這三個字，今天先在心裡說一次。

en.content:
You don't have to do more to deserve liking yourself.
You already are enough — you just haven't let yourself believe it yet.
en.editorNote: For anyone postponing self-acceptance until 'after I lose the weight,' 'after I get promoted,' 'after I save up.' That 'after' might never come. Try liking yourself today, as is. Just as an experiment.
variations: You're not lacking. You've just been measured with the wrong ruler.
Say 'I am enough' once in your head today. That's it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff's mindful self-compassion mantra; rephrased for everyday voice

zh-tw.content:
難過的時候，先不要急著想辦法。
承認「我現在真的不太好」，已經是照顧自己的一種。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「不行，我不能倒，我還有事要做」的撐著的人。你不是機器，難過的時候不用立刻修好。先承認、先停一下，再決定下一步。允許自己脆弱，是一種勇敢。
variations: 難過就難過一下，地球不會因此停轉。
「我不好」三個字，今天先准你說。

en.content:
When you're hurting, don't rush to fix it.
Just saying 'I'm not okay right now' counts as taking care of yourself.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps muscling through because 'I can't fall apart, I have things to do.' You're not a machine. You don't have to repair sadness on contact. Name it, pause, then decide. Letting yourself be fragile is a kind of brave.
variations: Be sad for a minute. The planet won't stop spinning.
'I'm not okay' is a full sentence. Today, you're allowed to use it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Glennon Doyle's 'boundaries don't limit your life, they expand it' theme; rephrased with a different metaphor

zh-tw.content: 界線不是把人推遠，是讓真正在乎你的人，知道怎麼靠近你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給把「設界線」誤會成「冷漠」的人。其實會因為你劃線就生氣的，本來就不是真的在乎你；那些願意尊重你界線的，才是值得留下來的人。界線是篩網，不是城牆。
variations: 劃線不是拒人於千里，是告訴別人哪裡是門。
界線是過濾器，留下的都是真的。

en.content:
Boundaries don't push people away.
They show the ones who actually care how to get close to you.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks setting limits makes them 'cold.' The people who get mad at your boundaries were never really there for you. The ones who respect them? Those are the keepers. A boundary is a filter, not a wall.
variations: Drawing a line isn't shutting people out. It's showing them where the door is.
Boundaries are a filter. Whoever's left is real.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Louise Hay's 'you've been criticizing yourself for years, try approving of yourself' theme; rephrased as a small experiment

zh-tw.content:
你已經罵了自己這麼多年，也沒有變得比較好。
換個方式試試看：今天就試著肯定自己一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 送給那個腦中永遠有一個聲音在挑剔的你。如果罵自己有用，你早就變成完美的人了——既然沒用，乾脆換個策略。把它當成 A/B test，肯定組做七天看看會不會比較舒服。
variations: 罵了這麼久都沒用，下一句要不要換成「你已經很努力了」？
對自己嚴格沒有讓你變更好，只是讓你變更累。

en.content:
You've been criticizing yourself for years, and it hasn't worked.
Try approving of yourself for a change — just today, just once.
en.editorNote: For the one with a constant nitpicking voice in your head. If self-criticism worked, you'd be perfect by now. Since it doesn't, run an A/B test: try the approval version for a week and see if it feels better.
variations: All that scolding hasn't fixed you. Try 'you're doing your best' next.
Being hard on yourself didn't make you better. It just made you tired.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff's 'honor and accept your humanness' framing; rephrased with a softer, more local voice

zh-tw.content: 你會累、會犯錯、會偶爾很沒用——這不是缺點，是你是個人的證據。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合最近因為一個小錯就在心裡演了三天小劇場的人。你不是 AI，不需要永遠正確。會累、會卡關、會有狀態不好的日子，這些都不是你壞掉了，只是你還活著。
variations: 犯錯不代表你失敗，只代表你今天是人類。
「我也會有狀態不好的日子」——這句話，你可以說。

en.content:
You get tired. You mess up. You feel useless sometimes.
That's not a flaw — that's proof you're human.
en.editorNote: For the person still replaying a small mistake from three days ago. You're not an AI. You don't have to be right all the time. Getting tired, getting stuck, having off days — none of that means you're broken. It means you're alive.
variations: Making mistakes doesn't mean you failed. It just means you're human today.
'I'm allowed to have off days' — say it out loud, it's true.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sharon Salzberg's 'you deserve your love and affection' theme; rephrased with a more direct, conversational frame

zh-tw.content:
你不需要先證明什麼，才值得自己的溫柔。
你光是今天有醒來、有試著過好這天，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「等我做到再說」的人。溫柔不是獎牌，不用先表現好才有資格領。今天有起床、有吃飯、有撐到現在，就是值得被自己好好對待的理由。把標準調低一點，不是放棄，是還你自由。
variations: 你不需要先表現好，才配得到自己的善意。
今天有撐到現在，就值得一句「辛苦了」。

en.content:
You don't have to prove anything first to deserve your own kindness.
Waking up today and trying — that's already enough.
en.editorNote: For the one who keeps saying 'I'll be kind to myself once I earn it.' Kindness isn't a trophy. You don't perform first to qualify. You got up, you ate something, you made it this far today — that's reason enough to treat yourself well. Lowering the bar isn't giving up. It's giving yourself room.
variations: You don't have to perform well to qualify for your own kindness.
You made it to now. That deserves a quiet 'good job.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Anna Taylor 'love yourself enough to set boundaries' theme; rephrased with a more direct Taiwanese voice

zh-tw.content:
會說「不」，是愛自己的第一句台詞。
別人不爽，是他們要處理的事，不是你的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給每次拒絕別人都會內疚一整晚的你。界線不是把人推開，是讓自己有地方站。你的時間、力氣都是有限的，學會分配，不是冷漠，是負責。
variations: 拒絕別人不會讓你變壞人，硬撐才會。
你不是 24 小時便利商店，沒有義務隨時為別人開著。

en.content:
Saying 'no' is the first line of loving yourself.
Other people's disappointment is theirs to handle, not yours.
en.editorNote: For the one who feels guilty for hours after turning someone down. Boundaries aren't walls — they're the floor you stand on. Your time and energy are finite. Spending them on purpose isn't cold, it's responsible.
variations: Saying no doesn't make you the villain. Burning out trying to please everyone does.
You're not a 24-hour convenience store. You don't owe anyone constant access.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'love yourself starting from accepting your imperfections' framing; rephrased with a softer, real-life voice

zh-tw.content:
不用等到變成更好的版本，才開始喜歡自己。
現在這個有點亂、有點怪、還在學的你，也值得被自己擁抱。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「等我瘦下來」「等我升職」「等我學會說不」才願意對自己好的人。喜歡自己不是獎品，不用達到某個門檻才能領。今天這個版本的你，已經夠資格。
variations: 你不是修圖前的廢稿，你是現在這個樣子的完成品。
等不到「更好的你」，因為你已經在這裡了。

en.content:
You don't have to wait until you're a better version to start liking yourself.
This messy, weird, still-learning you — also deserves a hug from you.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps saying 'once I lose the weight,' 'once I get the promotion,' 'once I learn to say no' — then I'll be kind to myself. Liking yourself isn't a prize you unlock. Today's version of you already qualifies.
variations: You're not a rough draft waiting to be edited. You're already a real thing.
Stop waiting for 'better you.' You're already here.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Maya Angelou 'you alone are enough, you have nothing to prove' theme; rephrased with a calmer everyday tone

zh-tw.content:
你不用證明給誰看，你才是夠的。
你光是存在，就已經是答案了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直在心裡跟看不見的人比賽的你。沒有人在評分。你不需要再多做一點、再厲害一點、再瘦一點，才有資格佔據這個位置。你在這裡，這件事本身就成立。
variations: 你不是來考試的，不用每天交成績單。
存在這件事不用申請，你已經有了。

en.content:
You don't have to prove anything to anyone to be enough.
You existing — that's already the answer.
en.editorNote: For the one who's been silently competing with people who aren't even watching. Nobody's grading you. You don't need to do more, be sharper, weigh less to earn your spot. You're here. That part is already settled.
variations: You're not here to take a test. You don't owe anyone a report card.
Existing isn't something you apply for. You already have it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'to be beautiful means to be yourself, you need to accept yourself' framing; rephrased with a more grounded local voice

zh-tw.content:
別人喜不喜歡你，是他們的事。
你喜不喜歡自己，才是你要回答的題目。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合最近被一句別人的評論卡住、睡不著的你。別人怎麼看你，你沒辦法控制，也不需要負責。把注意力收回來，看看現在的自己，問問：「我有沒有對得起這個人？」這題比較重要。
variations: 別人的看法是他的功課，不是你的。
把眼光從別人臉上收回來，看看自己。

en.content:
Whether other people like you — that's their thing.
Whether you like yourself — that's the question you have to answer.
en.editorNote: For the one losing sleep over one comment someone made. You can't control how others see you, and you don't have to. Pull your attention back to yourself and ask: am I treating this person — me — okay? That's the question that actually matters.
variations: Other people's opinions are their homework, not yours.
Take your eyes off their face. Look at yourself for a second.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Prentis Hemphill 'boundaries are the distance at which I can love you and me simultaneously'; rephrased into a more conversational Taiwanese voice

zh-tw.content:
界線不是把人推開。
是讓你還有力氣，繼續喜歡這個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種覺得「拒絕別人就是不夠愛他」的你。事實是相反的——一直勉強自己配合，反而會慢慢變成怨。畫一條線，是為了讓這段關係還能呼吸。
variations: 說不出口的「不要」，最後會變成藏在心裡的怨。
界線不是牆，是一個讓兩個人都還能呼吸的距離。

en.content:
A boundary isn't pushing someone away.
It's how you keep enough of yourself to still care about them.
en.editorNote: For when you've convinced yourself that saying no means you don't love them enough. It's actually the other way around — forcing yourself to say yes slowly turns into resentment. A line isn't a wall. It's the space that lets the relationship keep breathing.
variations: The 'no' you swallow today becomes the resentment you carry tomorrow.
A boundary isn't a wall. It's the space where both people can still breathe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff self-compassion practice ('this is a moment of suffering, may I be kind to myself'); rephrased with a grounded everyday tone

zh-tw.content:
今天很難，沒關係。
難的時候，就先對自己溫柔一點，其他的等等再說。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給今天什麼都不想做、躺著就在哭的你。不用急著振作，不用急著找方法。先承認「現在就是很難」，再給自己一杯熱的、一張被子、一句「你已經撐到現在了」。其他的，明天再處理。
variations: 難過不用解釋，也不用解決，先讓它在這裡。
你不是壞掉，你只是今天電量不夠。

en.content:
Today is hard. That's allowed.
When it's hard, be gentle with yourself first. Everything else can wait.
en.editorNote: For the day you can't get out of bed and you're crying for no clear reason. You don't have to bounce back. You don't have to fix it. Just admit it's hard, hand yourself a warm drink, a blanket, and a quiet 'you made it this far.' The rest is a tomorrow problem.
variations: Sadness doesn't need to be explained or solved. Let it sit there for a minute.
You're not broken. You're just running low on battery today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Womany article on self-acceptance vs. blind self-love; rephrased with a more honest, lived-in framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是覺得自己很完美。
是知道自己有缺點，還是願意站在自己這邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「我知道我有問題，但我還是討厭自己」的時刻。真正的自愛不是裝看不見缺點，是即使看見了，還是不會背叛自己。你可以一邊修，一邊愛。兩件事不衝突。
variations: 看見自己的缺點，還是選擇不離開自己。
你不用先變完美，才有資格站在自己這邊。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't thinking you're perfect.
It's knowing your flaws and still choosing to stand on your own side.
en.editorNote: For the 'I know what's wrong with me and I still hate myself' moments. Real self-love isn't pretending the flaws aren't there. It's seeing them and refusing to abandon yourself anyway. You can be working on yourself and loving yourself at the same time. Those two things don't cancel each other out.
variations: Seeing your flaws and choosing not to leave yourself anyway.
You don't have to become perfect before you're allowed to be on your own team.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Louise Hay 'you have been criticizing yourself for years and it hasn't worked, try approving of yourself'; rephrased with a more direct, Taiwanese spoken tone

zh-tw.content:
你已經罵自己很多年了，沒有變更好對不對？
那要不要試試看，換一種方式對自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合「我就是要逼自己才會進步」的那種人。事實是，你已經逼了二十年、三十年，你還是會在意同樣的事、卡在同樣的地方。或許不是你逼不夠，是這個方法本來就沒效。試試對自己好一點，反正你已經試過不好了。
variations: 罵自己沒讓你變更好，那就試試別的方法。
你對自己最嚴格，結果你還是最不快樂的那個。

en.content:
You've been beating yourself up for years now. Has it actually worked?
Maybe it's time to try being on your own side instead.
en.editorNote: For the 'I have to be hard on myself or I won't improve' crowd. The truth is — you've been hard on yourself for twenty, thirty years, and you're still stuck on the same things. Maybe you're not being hard enough isn't the issue. Maybe the method itself doesn't work. Try kindness. You've already tried the other thing.
variations: If yelling at yourself worked, you'd be done by now.
You're the harshest critic in your life — and somehow also the unhappiest. Connect the dots.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the Buddha-attributed 'you yourself deserve your love and affection' line; rephrased with a more grounded, daily-life framing

zh-tw.content:
你找遍全世界，也找不到一個比你更值得你疼愛的人。
那個人，從一開始就是你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「我好像對誰都很好，就是對自己最差」的時刻。你願意花時間幫朋友想生日禮物、記得另一半喜歡的咖啡、回訊息回到半夜——卻常常忘了問自己今天累不累。把那份用力，留一份給自己。
variations: 你對全世界都很溫柔，輪到自己的時候，也別跳過。
你疼愛的名單，記得把自己也寫上去。

en.content:
You could search the whole world and not find anyone more deserving of your love than you.
That person was you the entire time.
en.editorNote: For those 'I'm kind to everyone except myself' moments. You spend hours picking the right birthday gift for a friend, remember your partner's coffee order, reply to messages until midnight — and forget to ask yourself if you're tired. Save some of that effort for you.
variations: You're gentle with the whole world. Don't skip yourself.
Put your own name on the list of people you take care of.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my worth does not have to be earned' affirmation; rephrased with a more Taiwanese spoken cadence

zh-tw.content:
你的價值，不需要靠表現去換。
你不用先做到什麼，才有資格被愛。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種「我覺得我要更努力才會被喜歡」的疲憊時刻。你不是公司的 KPI，不需要每季交出成績單才有人疼你。你光是存在，就已經有人在乎了——包括你自己。
variations: 你不是業績，沒有達標也還是你。
被愛這件事，不是獎金，不用拼到崩潰才拿得到。

en.content:
Your worth isn't something you earn.
You don't have to achieve anything first to deserve being loved.
en.editorNote: For the 'I have to do more to be liked' kind of exhaustion. You're not a quarterly report. You don't owe anyone numbers in order to be cared about. You exist — that's already enough for someone to love you. Yourself included.
variations: You're not a sales target. Missing quota doesn't make you less of a person.
Love isn't a bonus you have to grind for.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Osho's 'you are already a masterpiece' framing; rephrased with a more honest, dual-truth tone

zh-tw.content:
你可以同時是一幅完成的畫，也是一張還沒畫完的草稿。
兩件事不衝突。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我都還沒變好，怎麼可以先愛自己」的朋友。你不用先變成最終版才值得喜歡。現在這個半成品的你，已經夠真、夠完整。修改中跟值得被愛，從來不是二選一。
variations: 你還在進步，不代表現在的你不夠好。
半成品也是品，不是廢品。

en.content:
You can be a finished masterpiece and an unfinished sketch at the same time.
Those two things don't cancel each other out.
en.editorNote: For the friend who says 'I'm not even fixed yet, how can I love myself?' You don't have to reach the final version to be loveable. The half-finished you right now is already real, already whole. Being a work in progress and being worthy of love were never on opposite sides.
variations: Still growing doesn't mean not enough yet.
A work in progress is still a work — not a waste.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'it is OK to rest and take time for myself' affirmation; rephrased with a more direct, anti-hustle tone

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，是讓你繼續走下去的方式。
你不是機器，本來就不該全年無休。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放在那種「我休息一下就會有罪惡感」的人手機桌布。你不是 7-11，不需要 24 小時亮著燈。允許自己今天什麼都不做，明天才有力氣繼續。會壞掉的不是進度，是你自己。
variations: 今天不做事，不會讓你變成廢物。
你的電池就是會沒電，這不是缺陷，是設計。

en.content:
Resting isn't slacking. It's how you keep going.
You're not a machine. You were never meant to run 24/7.
en.editorNote: Set this as a lock screen for anyone who feels guilty about resting. You're not a 7-Eleven. You don't have to keep the lights on around the clock. Let yourself do nothing today so you have something left for tomorrow. The thing that breaks isn't the schedule — it's you.
variations: Doing nothing today doesn't make you useless.
Your battery runs out. That's not a flaw — that's the design.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 viral IG healing caption 'I am my own safe space'; rephrased with concrete home-base framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用一直去別人那邊找安全感。
你自己，就是那個可以隨時回去的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「沒人秒回就開始慌、沒人陪就坐立難安」的朋友。安全感不是別人發的票，是你自己練出來的本領。從今天起，試著在心裡幫自己留一個座位。
variations: 別把自己的安全感，掛在別人的已讀上。
你才是自己的避風港，不是過夜的旅館。

en.content:
You don't have to keep going to other people to feel safe.
You're the place you can come back to anytime.
en.editorNote: For the friend who panics when no one replies fast and can't sit still without company. Safety isn't a ticket someone hands you — it's a skill you grow. Starting today, save yourself a seat inside your own head.
variations: Don't hang your safety on someone else's read receipt.
You're your own harbor. Not a motel for the night.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'you have to love yourself before asking others to love you'; rephrased with a less demanding tone

zh-tw.content:
不是要先愛自己才有資格被愛，
是先學會疼自己，你才知道別人對你好不好是什麼樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我都不愛我自己，誰會愛我」陷入自責的人。重點不是先達到某種標準才值得被愛——而是你對自己的方式，會變成你接受別人對你的底線。先把那條線拉高一點。
variations: 你對自己怎麼樣，就是你願意收下的最低標準。
別人怎麼對你，部分是看你怎麼對自己。

en.content:
It's not that you have to love yourself first to deserve love.
It's that once you treat yourself well, you finally recognize what being treated well looks like.
en.editorNote: For the spiral of 'if I don't love myself, who will?' The point isn't hitting some bar before you qualify — it's that how you treat yourself becomes the floor for what you'll accept from others. Raise the floor.
variations: How you treat yourself is the minimum you're willing to accept from others.
How people treat you is partly a copy of how you treat yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by QuoteFancy 2026 update 'I accept myself completely, just as I am' affirmation; rephrased with an honest, non-toxic-positivity framing

zh-tw.content:
接納自己，不是說服自己「我超棒」。
是承認「我有時候很糟」，然後還是不離開自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給對「正能量金句」過敏的你。真正的自愛不是每天逼自己唸三遍「我值得我配」——是清楚知道自己今天很爛、很煩、很沒用，然後依然選擇陪自己過完這一天。
variations: 接納不是粉飾，是看清楚還願意留下來。
「我今天就是不行」也是一種誠實，不是失敗。

en.content:
Accepting yourself isn't convincing yourself you're amazing.
It's admitting you're sometimes a mess and choosing not to leave anyway.
en.editorNote: For anyone allergic to toxic-positivity affirmations. Real self-love isn't chanting 'I am worthy' three times a day. It's knowing you're being annoying, useless, and a little broken today — and still deciding to stick around for yourself.
variations: Acceptance isn't a coat of paint. It's seeing the truth and staying.
'I'm just not okay today' is honesty, not failure.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love affirmations roundup 'I am learning to be gentle with myself'; rephrased with a learning-curve framing

zh-tw.content:
對自己好，是一種還在學的事。
學不會的那幾天，也別急著扣自己的分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我都看了這麼多自愛文章，怎麼還是會討厭自己」的疲憊星期天。自愛不是技能解鎖型的事，沒有通關。今天又回到罵自己的模式，不是退步，是這個練習本來就會反覆。
variations: 今天又對自己很兇，沒關係，明天重練。
練習的意思就是會失敗，不會失敗的叫表演。

en.content:
Being good to yourself is something you're still learning.
On the days you forget how, don't dock yourself points.
en.editorNote: For the tired Sunday of 'I've read so many self-love posts and I still hate myself.' Self-love isn't a skill you unlock once. There's no final boss. Sliding back into self-criticism today isn't backsliding — this practice is supposed to loop.
variations: Rough with yourself again today? Fine. Reset tomorrow.
Practice means it goes wrong. The thing that never fails is called a performance.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 kintsugi philosophy revival ('treat your scars like gold seams'); rephrased with concrete repair metaphor

zh-tw.content:
你身上那些被修補過的地方，
才是你身上最金、最亮的部分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總是想把過去的傷藏起來、覺得「破過就不值錢」的朋友。金繕的器皿之所以珍貴，不是因為它沒裂過，是因為它裂過還被好好修過。你也是。
variations: 別把裂痕當缺陷，那是你被修過的證明。
完好如初的人不珍貴，修補過還願意繼續用的才是。

en.content:
The places on you that were mended
are the most gold, the most luminous parts of you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps hiding old wounds, convinced 'once broken, less worth.' A kintsugi bowl isn't precious because it never cracked — it's precious because it cracked and someone took the time to mend it carefully. Same for you.
variations: Don't read the cracks as flaws. Read them as proof you got repaired.
Untouched isn't valuable. Mended-and-still-being-used is.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG caption trend 'less perfection, more acceptance'; rephrased with anti-curation Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
今年的目標：少一點完美，多一點接受。
你不是要被修圖的素材，你是會呼吸的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種發 IG 前要修十張照片、回訊息前要改五次語氣的朋友。完美沒有終點，但接受可以從今天開始。少花一點時間裝得很好，多花一點時間真的活著。
variations: 完美是別人在看你，接受是你在看自己。
今天不修圖，也允許自己不修心情。

en.content:
This year's goal: less perfection, more acceptance.
You're not raw material waiting to be edited. You're a person who breathes.
en.editorNote: For the friend who edits ten versions of one photo before posting and rewrites every message five times. Perfection has no finish line. Acceptance can start today. Spend less time looking polished, more time actually living.
variations: Perfection is them watching you. Acceptance is you watching yourself.
No filter today. And no edit on your mood either.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'the glow-up starts within' IG trend; rephrased with anti-external-fix framing

zh-tw.content:
真正的 glow up 不是換髮型、瘦五公斤、買新衣服，
是哪天你開始不會在心裡偷偷罵自己了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直靠外在改造想感覺自己變好的朋友。新眉毛、新濾鏡、新衣服都救不了那個心裡一直碎念自己的聲音。真的閃，是你不再每天都跟自己過不去。
variations: 外表發光容易，心裡安靜才難。
改造的順序錯了：先改腦中那個批評你的聲音。

en.content:
The real glow-up isn't a new haircut, five pounds lost, or a new outfit.
It's the day you stop quietly insulting yourself in your own head.
en.editorNote: For the friend chasing 'better' through one external upgrade after another. New brows, new filters, new clothes — none of it shuts up the voice nitpicking you from the inside. Real shine is when you stop being at war with yourself all day.
variations: Glowing on the outside is easy. Quiet on the inside is the hard part.
You've been renovating in the wrong order. Start with the voice in your head.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG short caption trend 'soft era, not weak era'; rephrased with reframing tone

zh-tw.content:
對自己溫柔，不是變軟弱。
是你終於知道，不用每天跟自己打架。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「對自己嚴格才會強」的朋友。溫柔不是放棄，是你不再把每天的力氣都拿來罵自己。省下來的那部分，才是你拿來做事的力氣。
variations: 溫柔是省下來的力氣，不是失去的力氣。
你不是變軟，你只是不再內耗了。

en.content:
Being gentle with yourself isn't getting weaker.
It's finally figuring out you don't have to fight yourself every day.
en.editorNote: For the friend who believes 'hard on yourself = strong.' Gentle isn't giving up. It's not spending all your daily energy on self-criticism. The energy you save? That's the energy you actually get things done with.
variations: Gentleness is saved energy, not lost energy.
You're not going soft. You're just done fighting yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-compassion theme 'saying no is reverence for your boundaries'; rephrased with conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
說「不」不是把人推開，
是在告訴自己：我也很重要。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一拒絕別人就會內疚一整天的朋友。拒絕不是壞，是你終於把自己也列進「值得被尊重的人」名單裡。第一次很難，之後會越來越自然。
variations: 你不是難搞，你只是不再委屈自己了。
每說一次「不」，都是在替自己留一塊位子。

en.content:
Saying no isn't pushing people away.
It's telling yourself: I matter too.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels guilty all day after turning someone down. A no isn't mean — it's you finally adding yourself to the list of people who deserve respect. The first one's hard; it gets easier.
variations: You're not difficult. You just stopped shortchanging yourself.
Every no you say keeps a seat reserved for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'rest is not lazy, it's self-care and essential' self-compassion framing; rephrased with anti-guilt angle

zh-tw.content:
累了就休息，這不叫懶。
手機沒電都知道要充，你也是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一停下來就覺得自己在偷懶、休息到一半還在想待辦事項的人。休息不是獎品，是維修。電充飽了，後面才跑得動。
variations: 你不是機器，本來就不該 24 小時運轉。
今天的躺平，是明天能站著的本錢。

en.content:
Tired? Rest. That's not lazy.
Even your phone gets to recharge — so do you.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels like a slacker the moment they stop, still mentally running the to-do list mid-break. Rest isn't a prize; it's maintenance. Charge fully now so you can actually go later.
variations: You're not a machine. You were never meant to run 24/7.
Today's lying flat is the deposit for tomorrow's standing tall.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 蔡素芬-style '善待自己實有必要' + 'self is your lifelong companion' framing; rephrased into a single line

zh-tw.content:
你這輩子陪自己最久。
對這個一直在的人，好一點不過分吧？
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友很大方、對自己很小氣的人。別人會來會走，只有你從頭到尾都在。對一個會陪你到最後的人好一點，再合理不過。
variations: 別人是過客，你是常住居民，先把自己照顧好。
你對外人都那麼好了，留一點那種好給自己。

en.content:
You're the one who's with you the longest.
Being kind to the person who never leaves — that's not too much to ask.
en.editorNote: For the person who's generous with friends but stingy with themselves. Everyone else comes and goes; you're the only one there start to finish. Be good to the one who stays till the end.
variations: Others are guests. You're the permanent resident — take care of you first.
You're that kind to strangers. Save a little of it for yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 IG short caption 'loving myself is my full-time job'; rephrased with playful Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
愛自己是我的正職，
讓別人滿意只是偶爾接的案子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當 IG 限動或個人簡介。重點是排序：先把自己顧好，再決定要不要去討好誰。把人生的主軸放回自己身上，別人的滿意只是加減賺。
variations: 你不是來給人打分數的，你是來過自己日子的。
讓自己開心是本業，討好別人最多算兼差。

en.content:
Loving myself is my full-time job.
Keeping others happy is freelance, at most.
en.editorNote: Great for a Story or a bio line. The point is the order: handle yourself first, then decide whether to please anyone else. Put your life's main thread back on you — other people's approval is just side income.
variations: You're not here to be graded. You're here to live your own life.
Keeping yourself happy is the main gig. Pleasing others is a side hustle at best.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 '愛自己就是做你自己，面對並接受自己的好與不好'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己最簡單的方法，
就是把好的壞的都認下來，然後說一句：嗯，這就是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只想把缺點藏起來、只敢給別人看好的一面的朋友。自愛不是只挑喜歡的部分留下，是連那些不太喜歡的也願意承認、一起帶著走。
variations: 你不用先變成更好的人，才開始接受現在的自己。
好的壞的都是你，不用拆開賣。

en.content:
The simplest way to love yourself
is to own all of it — the good and the bad — and say: yeah, that's me.
en.editorNote: For the friend who hides their flaws and only lets people see the polished side. Self-love isn't keeping only the parts you like — it's admitting the rest too, and carrying all of it along.
variations: You don't have to become a better person first to accept the one you are now.
The good and the bad are both you. No need to sell them separately.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 素黑 '先吃好一頓飯，睡好一個覺，不問理由地先強壯自己的身體'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
心情很差的時候，先別急著想通。
吃一頓好的，睡飽一覺，把身體顧好，腦袋自然會跟上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一遇到事就拼命想「我到底哪裡做錯」的人。有時候不是你想不開，是你太累太餓了。先把身體照顧好，很多事隔天就沒那麼嚴重了。
variations: 想不通的時候，問題常常出在你還沒睡飽。
先把自己餵飽睡飽，再來解人生難題也不遲。

en.content:
When you feel terrible, don't rush to figure it all out.
Eat something good, sleep it off, take care of your body — your head will catch up.
en.editorNote: For the person who immediately spirals into 'what did I do wrong' the moment something goes off. Sometimes you're not stuck — you're just exhausted and hungry. Handle the body first; a lot of things shrink overnight.
variations: When you can't think straight, the real problem is usually that you haven't slept enough.
Feed yourself, rest up, then tackle the big questions. They'll wait.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-compassion framing 'give yourself the same kindness you'd give others'; rephrased into a single conversational line

zh-tw.content:
你願意給別人的體諒，
也分一點給自己吧。
你又不是不值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對別人犯錯超寬容、對自己犯錯卻記恨好幾年的人。你給別人的那份「沒關係，誰都會這樣」，自己其實也用得上。別把溫柔都送光了。
variations: 你對別人那麼好，留一點那種好給自己不過分。
別人犯錯你說沒關係，輪到自己也說一次。

en.content:
The understanding you'd hand to someone else —
save a little for yourself.
It's not like you don't deserve it.
en.editorNote: For the person who forgives everyone else's mistakes but holds grudges against their own for years. That 'it's okay, anyone would' you give to others? You could use some too. Don't give all your softness away.
variations: You're that kind to everyone else. Keeping a little for yourself isn't asking too much.
When others mess up you say it's fine. Say it to yourself for once.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 劉冠吟 '獨一無二、可愛又可惡的你，值得你花一生好好陪伴'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
可愛的你、難搞的你、半夜亂想的你，
都是同一個你。
這個人，值得你花一輩子好好相處。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只想留下自己「好相處」那一面的人。你不是只有乖巧那部分才值得被愛，連那些麻煩、彆扭、想太多的部分，也一起被你收下，才叫真的陪自己。
variations: 你不是只有完美的時候才值得喜歡。
難搞也是你的一部分，不用偷偷藏起來。

en.content:
The lovable you, the difficult you, the one who overthinks at 2 a.m. —
all the same person.
And that person is worth spending a lifetime getting along with.
en.editorNote: For the person who only wants to keep their 'easy to be around' side. You're not worth loving only when you're agreeable — taking in the messy, awkward, overthinking parts too is what really counts as keeping yourself company.
variations: You're not only worth liking on your perfect days.
Being difficult is part of you too. No need to hide it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
我願意現在就接受自己，
而不是等到變成更好的版本才肯。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種把「等我做到⋯⋯再來喜歡自己」掛在嘴邊的人。接受不是放棄成長，是先停止用條件交換愛。你可以一邊進步，一邊現在就喜歡自己。
variations: 接受自己不是終點，是讓你能好好往前走的起點。
別把對自己的好，設成需要解鎖的成就。

en.content:
I accept myself right now,
not once I've become some better version.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps saying 'I'll like myself once I've achieved...' Acceptance isn't giving up on growth — it's just refusing to trade love for conditions. You can keep improving and like yourself today.
variations: Accepting yourself isn't the finish line. It's the starting point that lets you move forward.
Stop setting your own kindness as an achievement you have to unlock.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'treat yourself with the same compassion you show others' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你願意對別人那麼有耐心，
也記得把同樣的耐心，留一份給自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友的失誤超寬容、對自己卻零容忍的人。你不會因為朋友跌倒就罵他活該，那為什麼對自己這麼狠？把那份溫柔，分一點回來給自己。
variations: 你對別人的好，自己也值得收到一份。
對自己有耐心一點，你也是新手第一次當你自己。

en.content:
You're so patient with everyone else.
Keep a little of that patience for yourself too.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly forgiving of friends' slip-ups but has zero tolerance for their own. You wouldn't tell a friend they deserved to fall — so why be so harsh with yourself? Send some of that softness back your way.
variations: The kindness you give others — you deserve a share of it too.
Go easy on yourself. It's your first time being you, too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 '自我價值重建：從破碎到再站起來' article framing; rephrased into conversational line

zh-tw.content:
破碎過的你，不是壞掉了。
是被生活拆開過，現在正在用自己的方式重新拼回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給剛經歷低潮、覺得自己「回不去了」的朋友。重建不是要你變回原本那個樣子，是讓你長出更貼合現在的形狀。裂縫不是缺陷，是光透進來的地方。
variations: 你不是碎掉的，你只是還在重組。
拼回來的你，會比原本更知道哪些零件值得留。

en.content:
Having been broken doesn't mean you're damaged.
Life took you apart, and now you're putting yourself back together your own way.
en.editorNote: For a friend coming out of a low point who feels they 'can't go back to who they were.' Rebuilding isn't about returning to the old shape — it's growing into one that fits who you are now. The cracks aren't flaws. They're where the light gets in.
variations: You're not broken. You're just still reassembling.
The you that comes back together will know better which pieces were worth keeping.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '愛自己是終生浪漫的開始' Oscar Wilde framing in 2026 roundup; rephrased with concrete daily angle

zh-tw.content:
跟自己談一場戀愛吧。
那是唯一一個，保證不會分手的對象。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合單身但不想被「該脫單了」催促的人。一個人吃好吃的、帶自己去看海、買花給自己——這些不是將就，是你正在跟一個會陪你最久的人約會。
variations: 你才是那個會陪你走到最後的人，對他好一點。
別人會來會走，只有你從頭陪到尾。

en.content:
Try falling in love with yourself.
It's the one relationship that's guaranteed never to break up.
en.editorNote: For anyone single but tired of being nagged to 'find someone.' Taking yourself to a good meal, to see the ocean, buying yourself flowers — that's not settling. It's dating the person who'll stick around the longest.
variations: You're the one who stays till the end. Be good to them.
People come and go. You're the one who's there the whole way.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I am worthy of kindness, my existence is a blessing'; rephrased with conversational warmth

zh-tw.content:
你不需要做出什麼了不起的事，
才有資格被溫柔對待。
光是你在這裡，就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我要先有成就，才值得被好好對待」的人。被善待不是獎品，不用先達標才能領。你今天什麼都沒完成，也一樣值得一句溫柔的話。
variations: 被善待不是要先賺到的東西，是你本來就配的。
你不用先變得有用，才值得被好好對待。

en.content:
You don't have to do something impressive
to qualify for being treated gently.
Just being here is enough.
en.editorNote: For the person who believes 'I need to achieve something before I deserve good treatment.' Kindness isn't a prize you collect after hitting a target. You got nothing done today, and you still deserve a gentle word.
variations: Being treated well isn't something you earn first. It was always yours.
You don't have to be useful first to deserve being treated well.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '愛自己不需要理由，也不必與任何人比較' 2026 self-love roundup; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不需要理由，
也不需要先贏過誰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種要先「比別人好一點」才敢喜歡自己的朋友。喜歡自己不是排名賽，不用等到第一名才發獎。你現在這個版本，就有資格被自己喜歡。
variations: 別人怎麼活是別人的事，你過得好不好只跟自己比。

en.content:
Loving yourself doesn't need a reason —
and it definitely doesn't need you to outrank anyone.
en.editorNote: For the friend who has to be 'a bit better than others' before they'll like themselves. Liking yourself isn't a ranked match — you don't wait for first place to hand out the prize. This version of you already qualifies.
variations: How others live is their business. Whether you're doing okay is only measured against yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
我接受現在的自己，
也留一點空間給還在長大的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一邊嫌自己不夠好、一邊又逼自己進步的人。接受現在的你跟想變更好，這兩件事可以同時成立。先接住自己，再慢慢往前走。
variations: 你不用先變完美才能開始喜歡自己，邊走邊愛就好。

en.content:
I accept who I am right now,
and leave room for the me that's still growing.
en.editorNote: For anyone who criticizes themselves and pushes themselves to improve at the same time. Accepting who you are today and wanting to grow can both be true. Catch yourself first, then move forward slowly.
variations: You don't have to be perfect before you start liking yourself. Love yourself as you go.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '當你對自己溫柔，世界的尖銳也會變得柔軟' 2026 self-love roundup; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
當你願意對自己溫柔一點，
你會發現，世界的稜角也沒有想像中那麼尖。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合最近覺得「為什麼大家都對我這麼兇」的日子。有時候世界沒變，是你先對自己鬆了口氣，看出去的東西就柔和了。先從對自己好開始。
variations: 你怎麼對自己，多少會改變你怎麼看這個世界。

en.content:
The moment you're willing to be a little gentler with yourself,
you notice the world's edges aren't as sharp as they looked.
en.editorNote: For the days you keep thinking 'why is everyone so hard on me.' Sometimes the world didn't change — you just eased up on yourself first, and everything outside softened too. Start with being kind to you.
variations: How you treat yourself quietly shapes how you see the whole world.
