items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I do not have to earn my self-worth; it is there simply because I exist'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不是考試考來的。
它從你出生那天就發給你了，不會被當掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我要做出成績才值得被喜歡」的人。價值不是獎學金，不用每學期重新申請。你存在，它就在，沒有及格線。
variations: 你不用考一百分才配被疼，六十分也配，零分也配。

en.content:
Your worth isn't something you passed a test for.
It was handed to you the day you were born, and it can't be revoked.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I have to produce results to deserve being liked.' Worth isn't a scholarship you reapply for each term. You exist, so it's there — no passing grade required.
variations: You don't need a perfect score to deserve kindness. A failing grade still qualifies.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'I allow myself to rest without guilt' self-care affirmation; rephrased with conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先請示誰。
累了就停，這是你身體的權利，不是你要請的假。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種休息一下就覺得自己在偷懶、躺著也躺得很有罪惡感的人。休息不是犯規，是維修。手機沒電都會自動省電了，你也可以。
variations: 電量剩 5% 還在工作的不是敬業，是在傷機。

en.content:
You don't need permission to rest.
When you're tired, stop. That's your body's right, not a leave request you have to file.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels lazy the moment they pause, who can't even lie down without guilt. Rest isn't breaking the rules — it's maintenance. Even a phone goes into low-power mode. So can you.
variations: Working at 5% battery isn't dedication. It's damaging the device.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 'love yourself by being yourself, face and accept your good and bad' quote; rephrased into conversational line

zh-tw.content:
愛自己最簡單的方式，就是做你自己。
好的那面留著，不好的那面也別急著丟。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直想把自己「修剪」成別人喜歡的樣子的人。你的好跟不好是同一個人，刪掉一半就不是你了。先全部收下，再慢慢相處。
variations: 你不是要被編輯的草稿，你是完整的一本書。

en.content:
The simplest way to love yourself is to be yourself.
Keep the good parts — and don't rush to throw out the bad ones either.
en.editorNote: For anyone constantly pruning themselves into a shape other people prefer. Your good and your bad belong to the same person. Delete half and it's not you anymore. Take all of it in first, then get to know it slowly.
variations: You're not a draft to be edited. You're a whole book.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'self-love is in the details — eat well, sleep well, be kind to yourself' framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
愛自己沒那麼浪漫。
就是好好吃一頓飯、睡一個飽覺、不為難自己的身體。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「愛自己」要很儀式感、要去旅行要買花的人。其實它藏在最小的地方：今天有沒有好好吃飯、有沒有早點睡。先把基本款顧好，再談進階。
variations: 先吃飽睡好，自愛的高級課程之後再上。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't that romantic.
It's eating a proper meal, getting a full night's sleep, not punishing your own body.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'self-love' needs a ritual — a trip, fresh flowers. It actually hides in the smallest places: did you eat today, did you sleep early. Cover the basics first, then talk about the advanced stuff.
variations: Eat first, sleep well first. The advanced self-love course can wait.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I am proud of how far I have come' self-esteem framing; rephrased with conversational reflection angle

zh-tw.content:
偶爾回頭看看你走過的路。
那些你以為熬不過的，現在都成了背景了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只盯著「還沒做到的」、忘記自己已經走多遠的人。今天的你，是去年的你拼命撐出來的結果。回頭給她一個肯定，再往前走。
variations: 你不是原地踏步，你只是忙到忘了回頭看里程數。

en.content:
Look back at the road you've walked sometimes.
The things you thought you'd never get through are just background now.
en.editorNote: For the person who only stares at what they haven't done yet and forgets how far they've come. Today's you is what last year's you fought to build. Give her some credit, then keep walking.
variations: You're not stuck in place. You've just been too busy to check the mileage.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Cornell Sun piece on comparison culture and highlight reels; rephrased with concrete social-media framing

zh-tw.content:
你滑到的，是別人剪好的精華片段。
別拿你的幕後花絮，去比人家的正片。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種滑完限動就開始覺得自己過得很失敗的人。每個人 PO 出來的都是最好的那一面，你卻拿自己最累、最狼狽的時刻去比。比較的賽道從一開始就不公平，別跑了。
variations: 別人的限動是濾鏡，你的生活是原檔，本來就不一樣。
你看到的是預告片，不是他真正的人生。

en.content:
What you're scrolling through is someone's edited highlight reel.
Stop comparing your behind-the-scenes to their final cut.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels like a failure after a doom-scroll through Stories. Everyone posts their best angle, and you're stacking your most exhausted, messiest moments against it. The comparison track was never fair — stop running it.
variations: Their Stories have a filter. Your life is the raw file. Of course they don't match.
You're watching the trailer, not their actual life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Wondermind 'affirmations that won't make you feel like a fraud' angle; rephrased into believable self-talk framing

zh-tw.content:
如果「我超棒」聽起來太假，那就先從「我願意對自己好一點」開始。
小聲一點也算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種照著打氣語錄唸卻覺得很心虛、像在騙自己的人。自愛不用一步到位喊滿分，先承認「我願意試試看對自己好」就已經啟動了。誠實的小句子，比浮誇的大話有用。
variations: 不用一開始就愛到爆，先從不討厭自己開始。
你不用相信自己很完美，只要願意先不傷害自己。

en.content:
If "I'm amazing" sounds fake, start with "I'm willing to be a little kinder to myself."
Saying it quietly still counts.
en.editorNote: For anyone who recites pep-talk affirmations and just feels like they're lying. Self-love doesn't have to launch at a perfect ten — admitting 'I'm willing to try being good to myself' already starts it. An honest small line beats a grand fake one.
variations: You don't have to love yourself fully on day one. Start with not disliking yourself.
You don't have to believe you're perfect. Just be willing not to hurt yourself first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by June 2026 affirmation series themes of joy, curiosity and gentle self-love; rephrased with seasonal mid-year reset angle

zh-tw.content:
一年過一半了，沒做到的清單先放下。
你還活著、還願意往前，這件事本身就值得記一筆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合年中突然焦慮「目標都沒達成」的時候。六月不是用來清算前半年的，是用來深呼吸、重新出發的。沒完成的不代表失敗，只代表還在路上。
variations: 年中不是來算總帳的，是來換口氣的。
前半年沒走完沒關係，後半年還在你手上。

en.content:
Half the year's gone — put the unfinished list down.
You're still here, still willing to move forward. That alone is worth logging.
en.editorNote: For the mid-year panic of 'I haven't hit any of my goals.' June isn't for auditing the first half — it's for taking a breath and restarting. Unfinished doesn't mean failed. It means still in progress.
variations: Mid-year isn't for settling the accounts. It's for catching your breath.
So you didn't finish the first half. The second half is still in your hands.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup line '當你對自己溫柔，世界的尖銳也會變得柔軟'; rephrased into a gentleness-with-standards framing

zh-tw.content:
對自己溫柔，不代表對自己沒要求。
是就算你今天搞砸了，那個對你說話的聲音，還願意站在你這邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「溫柔」就是放縱、所以反而對自己更兇的人。溫柔不是不檢討，是檢討的時候不踩你一腳。你可以一邊進步，一邊還是自己的隊友。
variations: 教練會糾正你，但不會羞辱你，你對自己也該這樣。
對自己好不是放棄成長，是換一種不傷人的方式陪自己長大。

en.content:
Being gentle with yourself doesn't mean expecting nothing of yourself.
It means even on the day you blow it, the voice talking to you stays on your side.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'gentle' equals letting yourself off the hook, so they overcorrect into being harsh. Gentleness isn't skipping the review — it's not kicking yourself during it. You can keep growing and still be your own teammate.
variations: A good coach corrects you without humiliating you. Do the same for yourself.
Being kind to yourself isn't quitting on growth. It's a non-bruising way to grow up alongside yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 'loving yourself means facing and accepting your good and bad' quote; rephrased into a self-acceptance framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己沒有什麼祕訣，
就是把你好的那面跟爛的那面，一起認下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只想留下「完美自己」、想偷偷刪掉缺點的人。你不是只挑喜歡的部分簽收，整個你才是完整的你。連那些不順眼的地方一起認，才叫真的愛自己。
variations: 你不是套餐，不能只挑愛吃的，剩下的退掉。
接受自己的爛，不是認輸，是認領。

en.content:
There's no trick to loving yourself.
You just claim the good parts and the messy parts together.
en.editorNote: For the person who wants to keep only the 'perfect self' and quietly delete the flaws. You don't get to sign for only the parts you like — the whole you is the real you. Claiming the unflattering bits too is what loving yourself actually means.
variations: You're not a combo meal. You can't keep the good stuff and return the rest.
Accepting your mess isn't admitting defeat. It's claiming what's yours.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased into permission-to-be-a-work-in-progress framing

zh-tw.content:
你可以一邊不滿意自己，一邊還是好好對自己。
想變好跟愛自己，從來不是二選一。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我都還沒變好，憑什麼愛自己」的人。等到滿意才肯善待自己，那一天可能永遠不會來。可以一邊努力、一邊先把自己當人看。
variations: 想進步，不用先恨自己一頓當入場券。
施工中的房子也可以開暖氣，你也是。

en.content:
You can be unhappy with yourself and still treat yourself well.
Wanting to grow and loving yourself were never an either-or.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I haven't improved yet, so what right do I have to love myself?' If you wait until you're satisfied to be kind to yourself, that day may never come. You can keep working and still treat yourself like a human in the meantime.
variations: Wanting to improve doesn't require a round of self-loathing as the entry fee.
A house under construction can still turn the heat on. So can you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Beyoncé 'if there's one thing I'm willing to bet on, it's myself' quote in 2026 self-love roundup; rephrased into a quieter everyday-confidence framing

zh-tw.content:
全世界都還在觀望的時候，
至少你要當第一個押自己的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種等別人先肯定、才敢相信自己的人。沒人看好的時候最孤單，但也是你最該站出來挺自己的時候。你都不押自己，誰要替你下注？
variations: 別人按讚是加碼，你自己相信才是本金。
等別人先相信你？那你大概要等很久。

en.content:
While the whole world's still waiting to see,
be the first one to bet on yourself.
en.editorNote: For the person who waits for outside approval before daring to believe in themselves. The moment no one's rooting for you is the loneliest — and exactly when you should step up for you. If you won't back yourself, who's placing the bet?
variations: Other people's likes are a bonus. Your own belief is the principal.
Waiting for someone else to believe in you first? That's a long wait.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup line '愛自己不需要理由，也不必與任何人比較'; rephrased into a no-justification-needed framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不用先列出三個理由。
不用比誰瘦、比誰會賺、比誰過得好。
你就是你，這個理由已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我又沒特別厲害，憑什麼喜歡自己」的人。喜歡自己不是頒獎典禮，不用先有得獎事蹟。光是你存在這件事，就不需要任何附加條件。
variations: 別人都用條件愛你，你至少無條件愛自己一次。
你不是參賽作品，不用得名才值得被留下。

en.content:
Loving yourself doesn't require listing three reasons.
No comparing who's thinner, who earns more, who's living better.
You're you. That reason's already enough.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I'm not especially impressive, so why would I like myself?' Liking yourself isn't an awards ceremony — you don't need a list of achievements first. Just existing comes with no terms and conditions.
variations: Everyone else loves you on conditions. Love yourself unconditionally at least once.
You're not a contest entry. You don't have to place to be worth keeping.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 quote roundup line '這世上只有一種成功，就是用自己喜歡的方式過自己的人生'; rephrased into a self-defined-success framing

zh-tw.content:
成功不是活成別人羨慕的樣子，
是活成你早上願意起床的樣子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種照著別人的劇本拼命跑、回頭卻發現自己一點都不快樂的人。別人的成功範本不一定合你穿。用你喜歡的方式過日子，那才是只屬於你的成功。
variations: 活得讓人羨慕，不如活得讓自己甘願。
別人的人生勝利組，不一定是你的快樂組。

en.content:
Success isn't living a life others envy.
It's living a life you're willing to wake up for.
en.editorNote: For the person sprinting through someone else's script who looks back and realizes they're not happy at all. Other people's templates for success don't always fit you. Living your own way is the only success that's actually yours.
variations: Better to live a life you're at peace with than one others envy.
Someone else's 'winning at life' isn't necessarily your 'happy at life.'

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'your worth is not earned, it is inherent' healing affirmation; rephrased with a no-receipt framing

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不是賺來的，
所以也沒有人能把它扣回去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我要先做出成績，才有資格喜歡自己」的人。價值不是薪水，不會因為你今天沒產出就被打折。它出廠就附在你身上，不用打卡領取。
variations: 你不是靠表現換存在感的，你本來就在。
沒有業績的日子，你還是原價的你。

en.content:
Your worth was never earned,
so no one gets to deduct it back.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I have to produce results before I'm allowed to like myself.' Worth isn't a paycheck — it doesn't get discounted on the days you output nothing. It came installed. No clock-in required.
variations: You don't trade performance for existence. You were already here.
On the days with no results, you're still full price.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 楊丞琳 '了解我的人就了解我，不必為了討好別人改變自己' 2026 quote roundup; rephrased into a no-explaining framing

zh-tw.content:
懂你的人不用你解釋，
不懂你的人你解釋了也沒用。
所以省點力氣，先做你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總想把自己「說明清楚」、深怕被誤會的人。你不是一份要附使用說明書的產品。對的人會自己讀懂你，不對的人怎麼翻譯都會看錯頁。
variations: 你不是維基百科，沒義務讓每個人都查得懂你。
解釋給不懂的人聽，比對牛彈琴還累，牛至少不會反駁。

en.content:
The people who get you don't need the explanation.
The people who don't won't get it even after one.
So save your energy. Just be yourself.
en.editorNote: For the person who's always trying to 'explain themselves' out of fear of being misread. You're not a product that ships with a manual. The right people read you on their own; the wrong ones flip to the wrong page no matter the translation.
variations: You're not Wikipedia. You don't owe everyone a searchable version of you.
Explaining yourself to someone who won't get it is worse than talking to a wall. At least the wall doesn't argue back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'your pace is not slow, it is personal' and 'flowers need time to bloom, so do you' self-love quotes; rephrased into a single conversational line

zh-tw.content:
你不是慢，你只是按自己的時間在長。
花從來不會因為旁邊先開了，就覺得自己壞掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一打開社群就覺得「大家都跑在我前面」的人。別人開花的時候你可能在扎根，看起來沒動，其實一直在長。你的花期還沒到，不是你壞了。
variations: 你不是落後，你是還沒輪到你的季節。
別人先開花是別人的事，你的根還在偷偷變壯。

en.content:
You're not slow. You're growing on your own schedule.
A flower never thinks it's broken just because the one beside it bloomed first.
en.editorNote: For the person who opens social media and instantly feels 'everyone's ahead of me.' While others bloom, you might be rooting — looks like nothing, but it's still growth. Your season just hasn't come. You're not broken.
variations: You're not behind. It's just not your season yet.
Someone else blooming first is their business. Your roots are quietly getting stronger.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'you can be productive and rested, they are not opposites' self-love affirmation; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
休息不是努力的相反。
它是讓你能繼續努力的那一半。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一停下來就有罪惡感、覺得「休息＝偷懶」的人。手機沒電要充電，你也是。今天躺平不是放棄人生，是在幫明天的自己備電。
variations: 耍廢不是浪費，是替明天存電。
你不是機器，連機器都要關機散熱。

en.content:
Rest isn't the opposite of effort.
It's the half that lets you keep going.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty the moment they stop, who reads 'rest' as 'slacking.' Your phone needs charging; so do you. Lying flat today isn't giving up on life — it's storing battery for tomorrow-you.
variations: Doing nothing isn't waste. It's saving charge for tomorrow.
You're not a machine. Even machines power down to cool off.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 '愛自己就是面對並接受自己的好與不好' 2026 quote roundup; rephrased into a whole-package framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是只挑喜歡的部分愛，
是連那些你想關燈不看的地方，也願意留一盞。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只接受自己「好的一面」、把缺點偷偷藏起來的人。你不是只賣優點的精選輯，你是整張專輯，連那幾首跳過的歌也是你。整個收下，才叫真的愛。
variations: 你不是只能播放精選輯，B 面也是你。
愛自己是全收，不是只留得獎的那幾項。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't loving only the parts you like.
It's keeping one light on even in the corners you'd rather not look at.
en.editorNote: For the person who only accepts their 'good side' and quietly hides the flaws. You're not a greatest-hits compilation — you're the whole album, including the tracks you skip. Take all of it. That's what loving yourself means.
variations: You're not just the singles. The B-sides are you too.
Loving yourself is taking the whole package, not only the award-winning bits.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Morgan Harper Nichols 'you don't have to earn rest, peace, safety' 2026 self-compassion roundup; rephrased with concrete everyday framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用先把自己累垮，才有資格休息。
平靜、被善待、好好喘口氣，這些你一出生就有資格了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「等我做完這件事再休息」永遠排不到自己的人。休息不是發給累壞的人的獎章，是你本來就配得的東西。今天就可以領，不用等。
variations: 休息不是獎品，是配備。
你不用累到極限才准休息，現在就可以。

en.content:
You don't have to wreck yourself before you're allowed to rest.
Peace, kindness, a moment to breathe — you've qualified for all of it since the day you were born.
en.editorNote: For the person whose 'I'll rest after this one thing' never actually arrives. Rest isn't a medal handed to the exhausted — it's something you already deserve. You can claim it today, no waiting.
variations: Rest isn't a prize. It's standard equipment.
You don't have to hit your limit to earn rest. Now works.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased with outgrowing-people angle

zh-tw.content:
你可以長大到某些關係再也裝不下你。
那不是背叛，是你終於長到自己的尺寸了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種因為「離開會不會太狠」而捨不得放下舊關係的人。人會長大，圈子會換，這不是你變壞，是你變大了。穿不下的衣服不會怪你，舊關係也不該。
variations: 你變了不代表你錯了，可能只是你終於對了。
有些人只能陪你到某一段，謝謝就好，不用內疚。

en.content:
You're allowed to outgrow relationships that no longer fit you.
That's not betrayal — it's you finally growing into your own size.
en.editorNote: For the person who can't let go of an old connection because 'wouldn't leaving be cruel?' People grow, circles change. That's not you turning bad — it's you getting bigger. Clothes that don't fit don't resent you. Neither should old ties.
variations: Changing doesn't mean you got it wrong. Maybe you finally got it right.
Some people are only here for one chapter. Say thanks, skip the guilt.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sophia Bush 'you are allowed to be both a masterpiece and a work in progress' 2026 quote; rephrased into conversational dual-state framing

zh-tw.content:
你可以同時是「已經很好」跟「還在變更好」。
這兩件事不衝突，一直都同時存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我還沒變好，所以不配喜歡自己」的人。成品和半成品不是二選一，你可以一邊欣賞現在的自己，一邊繼續長。喜歡自己不用等到終點。
variations: 你不是要等完工才值得，你蓋到一半也很美。
已經夠好，跟還想更好，可以同時成立。

en.content:
You can be 'already enough' and 'still becoming' at the same time.
These two don't cancel out — they've always coexisted.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I'm not improved yet, so I don't get to like myself.' Finished and in-progress aren't either-or. You can admire who you are now while still growing. Liking yourself doesn't wait for the finish line.
variations: You don't have to be done to be worth it. You're beautiful half-built.
Already enough and still wanting more can both be true.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing line '允許自己慢慢前行，不必追趕世界的節奏'; rephrased into own-pace framing

zh-tw.content:
你不需要趕上世界的時速。
用自己走得穩的速度走，遲到的人生不存在。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種看別人都在前進、自己卻像原地踏步而焦慮的人。人生不是賽跑，沒有起跑槍也沒有終點線。慢慢走、走得穩，比衝得快又跌倒好太多。
variations: 你不是慢，你是在走一條只有你能走的路。
沒有人規定幾歲要到哪，那都是別人編的時刻表。

en.content:
You don't have to match the world's speed.
Go at the pace where your feet stay steady — there's no such thing as a late life.
en.editorNote: For the person who panics watching everyone move forward while they feel stuck in place. Life isn't a race — no starting gun, no finish line. Walking slow and steady beats sprinting and tripping every time.
variations: You're not slow. You're walking a road only you can walk.
No one decided what age you reach what. That timetable is made up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 素黑 '自愛是承認你不是完人' and '別跟自己過不去' 2026 self-love roundup; rephrased into a stop-fighting-yourself line

zh-tw.content:
自愛有時候只是一句很簡單的話：
「好啦，別再跟自己過不去了。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一件小事卡在心裡反覆責備自己好幾天的人。你已經對自己很嚴格了，今天可以放過自己一次。承認自己不是完人，不是認輸，是給自己鬆綁。
variations: 你最難放過的人，常常就是你自己。換今天放過一次。
別當自己的法官，當自己的隊友。

en.content:
Sometimes self-love is just one simple sentence:
"Okay — stop giving yourself such a hard time."
en.editorNote: For the person who lets one small thing replay in their head, scolding themselves for days. You've been strict enough on yourself. You can let it go this once. Admitting you're not perfect isn't surrender — it's loosening the rope.
variations: The person you forgive last is usually yourself. Make today the exception.
Stop being your own judge. Be your own teammate.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself the grace to grow'; rephrased into grace-while-growing framing

zh-tw.content:
我接受現在的自己，
同時給自己慢慢長大的空間。
這兩件事，從來不衝突。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「要先變好才能接受自己」的人。接受不是停在原地，成長也不是否定現在。你可以一邊喜歡此刻的自己，一邊往前走。給自己一點長大的餘地。
variations: 接受現在，不代表放棄變好；想變好，也不代表現在不夠好。
你可以一邊愛自己，一邊慢慢長大。

en.content:
I accept who I am right now,
and I give myself room to keep growing.
These two never cancel each other out.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'I have to fix myself before I can accept myself.' Acceptance isn't staying stuck, and growing isn't rejecting the now. You can like who you are today while still moving forward. Leave yourself some room to grow.
variations: Accepting now doesn't mean giving up on better; wanting better doesn't mean now isn't enough.
You can love yourself and grow up slowly at the same time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'My existence is a blessing, and I am worthy of kindness'; rephrased into worthy-of-your-own-kindness framing

zh-tw.content:
你值得被溫柔對待，
而第一個該這樣對你的人，是你自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對別人很客氣、對自己卻特別狠的人。你不需要先做對什麼，才配得上溫柔。光是你存在，就已經值得。今天先把那份善意留一份給自己。
variations: 你對全世界都很客氣，唯獨對自己很兇。換一下吧。
值得被善待這件事，不用等別人點頭。

en.content:
You deserve to be treated gently,
and the first person who should do that is you.
en.editorNote: For the person who's polite to everyone but brutal with themselves. You don't have to do anything right first to deserve kindness. Just existing already qualifies. Keep some of that gentleness for yourself today.
variations: You're courteous to the whole world and harsh only with yourself. Swap that.
Deserving kindness isn't something you wait for others to approve.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I release self-doubt and embrace self-love'; rephrased into letting-go-of-the-doubt framing

zh-tw.content:
今天放下那句「我是不是不夠好」。
它跟了你很久，但它從來沒說對過。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種做什麼都先質疑自己一輪的人。「我夠好嗎」這句話聽起來很謙虛，其實只是把你困在原地。它陪你很久，但它的判斷一直都是錯的。今天可以讓它下班。
variations: 懷疑自己不是謙虛，是慣性。今天試著不照它的劇本走。
那句「我不夠好」陪你很久，但它從沒幫過你。

en.content:
Today, set down the question 'am I not good enough.'
It's followed you a long time, and it's never once been right.
en.editorNote: For the person who questions themselves before doing anything. 'Am I enough?' sounds humble, but it just keeps you stuck. It's kept you company for years, yet its verdict has always been wrong. Let it clock out today.
variations: Self-doubt isn't humility, it's a habit. Try not following its script today.
That 'I'm not enough' line has been with you forever, and it's never once helped.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Today.com 'you are more than enough' self-love roundup; rephrased into stop-shrinking-yourself framing

zh-tw.content:
別再把自己縮小到剛好不打擾任何人的尺寸。
你本來就可以好好佔據你的位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種講話會先說「不好意思」、怕自己存在感太強的人。你不是來這世界把自己藏起來的。你可以有意見、可以被看見、可以理直氣壯地存在。別再為了配合別人而縮水。
variations: 你不是來道歉的，你是來活著的。
把自己縮小不會讓你比較被愛，只會讓你比較累。

en.content:
Stop shrinking yourself down to a size that disturbs no one.
You were always allowed to take up your own space.
en.editorNote: For the person who opens with 'sorry' and worries about being too much. You didn't come here to hide yourself. You're allowed to have opinions, be seen, exist without apology. Stop shrinking just to fit around everyone else.
variations: You're not here to apologize. You're here to live.
Making yourself smaller won't make you more loved, just more tired.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing affirmation 'I give myself the same compassion I give others'; rephrased into treat-yourself-like-someone-you-love framing

zh-tw.content:
你對別人的體諒，永遠用不完。
試著分一點點，留給今天的自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友超有耐心、對自己零容忍的人。同樣一件事，發生在朋友身上你會說「沒關係」，發生在自己身上卻罵半天。今天試著用對朋友的那套標準，對自己一次。
variations: 你給別人的那份理解，自己也用得上。
對朋友會說的那句「辛苦了」，今天也對自己說一次。

en.content:
Your patience for other people never runs out.
Try saving a little of it for today's you.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly patient with friends but has zero tolerance for themselves. The same thing happens to a friend and you say 'it's okay' — happens to you and you scold yourself for hours. Try using your friend-standard on yourself once today.
variations: That understanding you hand out to others? You could use some too.
The 'you did your best' you'd say to a friend — say it to yourself today.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'rest when tired, say no without over-explaining'; rephrased into permission-to-rest-without-a-defense framing

zh-tw.content:
累了就休息，不用先列一份理由清單。
你不是欠誰一份報告，才能停下來喘口氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種連休息都要先跟自己交代「我今天做了哪些事所以可以休」的人。你不需要證明自己累得夠格才能躺平。累了就是足夠的理由，不用附上證明。
variations: 拒絕別人不用解釋一整段，「我今天不行」就是完整的句子。
你不是請假要附證明的員工，累就是準假理由。

en.content:
Rest when you're tired — you don't owe anyone a list of reasons.
You're not in debt for a report just to catch your breath.
en.editorNote: For the person who has to justify resting by listing everything they did first. You don't need to prove you're tired enough to qualify for a break. Tired is reason enough — no evidence required.
variations: Saying no doesn't need a paragraph. 'I can't today' is a complete sentence.
You're not an employee who needs a doctor's note. Tired is the note.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you teach people how to treat you by deciding what you will and won't accept'; rephrased into self-respect-sets-the-standard framing

zh-tw.content:
別人怎麼對你，有一半是你教出來的。
你願意忍多少，他們就會給你多少。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直被當作「好說話」、什麼都往身上攬的人。你每一次的退讓，都在默默告訴對方「這樣對我沒關係」。想被好好對待，先從不再委屈自己開始。
variations: 你怎麼對自己，就是給別人的範本。
你接受的下限，會變成別人對你的標準。

en.content:
Half of how people treat you is something you taught them.
However much you'll tolerate is however much they'll hand you.
en.editorNote: For the person known as 'easy to deal with' who keeps absorbing everything. Every time you let it slide, you're quietly telling them 'this is fine.' Want to be treated well? Start by not shortchanging yourself.
variations: How you treat yourself is the template you hand everyone else.
The floor you'll accept becomes the standard people hold you to.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Anne Hathaway 'I stopped chasing perfect, it stopped being interesting' quote in 2026 roundup; rephrased into perfection-is-boring framing

zh-tw.content:
我早就不追求完美了。
那東西不只難拿，還很無聊——做你自己，比做對的人有趣多了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種出門前修圖修到沒辦法出門、做什麼都要剛剛好才敢拿出來的人。完美沒有那麼值得，你身上那些不對稱、不標準的地方，才是別人記得你的原因。
variations: 完美的東西大家看過就忘，有點瑕疵的才有記憶點。
你不是來當樣品的，你是來活得有趣的。

en.content:
I stopped chasing perfect a long time ago.
It's not just hard to reach — it's boring. Being yourself beats being flawless.
en.editorNote: For the person who edits a photo until they can't post it, who only shows things when they're exactly right. Perfect isn't worth that much. The uneven, off-spec parts of you are exactly what people remember.
variations: Flawless things get forgotten. The slightly cracked ones stick.
You're not here to be a sample product. You're here to live an interesting life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '你必須先愛自己，才有資格要求別人愛你' headline framing; rephrased into self-love-as-the-baseline framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用先愛自己，才有資格被別人愛。
但你先愛了自己，就不會再為了被愛而委屈自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一段感情裡一直退讓、怕對方離開所以什麼都答應的人。自愛不是談戀愛的入場券，而是你的底氣——有了它，你就不會把別人的喜歡看得比自己還重。
variations: 先把自己放在心上，別人才不會把你放在腳邊。
你愛自己的程度，決定了你願意忍受多少不被珍惜。

en.content:
You don't have to love yourself first to deserve someone's love.
But once you do, you'll stop shrinking yourself just to be loved.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps giving in inside a relationship, agreeing to everything out of fear they'll be left. Self-love isn't the ticket to being loved — it's your backbone. With it, you stop ranking someone's affection above yourself.
variations: Put yourself in your own heart first, so others can't keep you at their feet.
How much you love yourself decides how much being unappreciated you'll put up with.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 素黑 'self-love isn't a promise to never err, it's staying aware and moving forward' quote; rephrased into awareness-not-flawlessness framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是保證以後都不會再犯錯，
是犯錯的時候願意看清楚、原諒自己，然後再往前走一步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一搞砸就覺得「我又退步了」、開始懷疑自己根本沒在進步的人。自愛不是變成不會出錯的人，是出錯後不把自己丟掉，而是牽著自己繼續走。看見、接住、前進，這就夠了。
variations: 進步不是再也不跌倒，是跌倒後願意自己爬起來。
你不是在原地打轉，你是在學會怎麼對自己溫柔。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't a promise you'll never mess up again.
It's being willing, when you do, to see it clearly, forgive yourself, and take one more step forward.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels 'I've backslid again' after every slip and starts doubting they're growing at all. Self-love isn't becoming someone who never errs — it's not abandoning yourself when you do, and walking on hand in hand. See it, catch it, move forward. That's enough.
variations: Progress isn't never falling. It's being willing to get yourself back up.
You're not spinning in place. You're learning how to be gentle with yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Brianna Wiest 'love yourself enough to set boundaries, your time and energy are precious' framing; rephrased into time-as-finite-currency angle

zh-tw.content:
你的時間和力氣是會用完的，不是吃到飽。
捨得花在誰身上，自己要先選好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一被開口就答應、行程被別人塞滿的人。時間不是無限續杯，給了這個就給不了那個。先把自己排進行事曆，剩下的再說。
variations: 你的力氣不是公用插座，不是誰來都能充。
把時間留給消耗你的人，就沒空留給愛你的人了。

en.content:
Your time and energy run out — they're not all-you-can-eat.
Who gets them is a call you make first.
en.editorNote: For the person who says yes the second someone asks, whose calendar fills up with everyone else's stuff. Time isn't a bottomless refill — spend it here and it's gone for there. Put yourself on the schedule first.
variations: Your energy isn't a public outlet anyone can plug into.
Give your time to people who drain you and there's none left for the ones who love you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 'love yourself by being yourself, face and accept your good and bad' quote; rephrased into accepting-both-sides framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不是只挑喜歡的部分留下來。
是連那些你想藏起來的地方，也願意說一句：好，你也是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只敢給別人看好的一面、把脆弱和缺點全藏起來的人。真正的接受，不是把不喜歡的自己刪掉，是承認那也是你的一部分。完整地活著，比完美地演著輕鬆多了。
variations: 你不是只有得獎那一面，落選那一面也是你。
藏起來的那些，不會因為沒人看到就不是你。

en.content:
Loving yourself isn't keeping only the parts you like.
It's looking at the bits you'd rather hide and saying: okay, you're me too.
en.editorNote: For the person who only shows their good side and buries every flaw and soft spot. Real acceptance isn't deleting the self you dislike — it's admitting that's part of you too. Living whole is lighter than performing perfect.
variations: You're not just the award-winning side. The runner-up side is you too.
The parts you hide don't stop being you just because no one sees them.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'the only success is living life your own way' classic quote roundup; rephrased into self-defined-success framing

zh-tw.content:
這世上只有一種成功：
用你自己喜歡的方式，把日子過下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種被「你怎麼還沒結婚」、「同學都買房了」追著跑的人。別人的成功表跟你的人生根本不同張考卷。能過得像自己，就已經是別人羨慕不來的那種成功。
variations: 你不是來達成別人的待辦清單的。
活成別人眼中的成功，常常是離自己最遠的時候。

en.content:
There's only one kind of success:
living your days in the way that feels like you.
en.editorNote: For the person chased by 'why aren't you married yet' and 'everyone our age owns a place.' Their scorecard and your life aren't even the same exam. Living like yourself is the kind of success no one can copy off you.
variations: You're not here to finish someone else's to-do list.
Becoming someone else's idea of success is often the furthest you'll be from your own.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I am deserving of love and abundance, just as I am right now' affirmation; rephrased into worthiness-without-conditions framing

zh-tw.content:
你值得好的東西，不附帶任何條件。
不是等你瘦下來、升上去、變更好，才開始值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直把「值得」設成解鎖任務的人——等我考上、等我賺到、等我變好看再說。你的價值不是會員等級，不用集點。現在這個版本，就已經有資格被好好對待了。
variations: 你不是達標才有獎，你本來就有領獎資格。
別把「值得」設成下一個目標，它一直都在你身上。

en.content:
You deserve good things — no conditions attached.
Not once you're thinner, promoted, or improved. Now.
en.editorNote: For the person who keeps setting 'deserving' as a quest to unlock — once I pass, once I earn it, once I look better. Your worth isn't a membership tier you collect points for. This version of you already qualifies to be treated well.
variations: You don't win the prize by hitting a target. You were always eligible.
Stop filing 'worthy' under your next goal. It's already on you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'self-love is treating yourself well, not denying yourself' theme; rephrased into kind-inner-voice-as-daily-practice framing

zh-tw.content:
善待自己不用很盛大。
累的時候不催自己、做不到的時候不罵自己，這樣就很夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「對自己好」要花大錢、要去旅行、要做很多事的人。其實最日常的善待，是你跟自己說話的語氣。今天不逼自己、不貶自己，已經是很實在的疼愛了。
variations: 對自己好，先從不對自己兇開始。
善待自己不是犒賞，是每天少傷自己一點。

en.content:
Being kind to yourself doesn't need to be grand.
Not rushing yourself when you're tired, not scolding yourself when you fall short — that's plenty.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'being good to yourself' means spending big, taking a trip, doing a lot. The most everyday kindness is the tone you use with yourself. Not pushing, not putting yourself down today — that's already real care.
variations: Being good to yourself starts with not being harsh to yourself.
Kindness to yourself isn't a reward. It's hurting yourself a little less each day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 update 'self-love begins when self-judgment ends' viral IG line; rephrased with concrete reframing

zh-tw.content: 愛自己，是從你停止當自己的法官那一刻開始的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種腦中有個小法官，整天在判你「這樣不對、那樣不夠好」的人。你不是被告，也沒犯什麼罪。把那個法袍脫了，今天先當自己的辯護人就好。
variations: 你腦中那場庭審，可以休庭了。
別當自己的檢察官，當自己的隊友。

en.content: Loving yourself begins the moment you stop being your own judge.
en.editorNote: For the person with a tiny judge in their head ruling all day that 'this is wrong, that's not good enough.' You're not on trial and you haven't committed anything. Hang up the robe — today, just be your own defense.
variations: That trial in your head? You can call a recess.
Stop being your own prosecutor. Be your own teammate.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 '365 days of self-love' daily-practice affirmation series; rephrased into a small-steady-deposit framing

zh-tw.content:
愛自己不用一次到位。
每天存一點點，一年後你會發現自己變有底氣了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我就是不會愛自己」而放棄的人。它不是一筆要立刻付清的大錢，是每天投幾塊進去的零錢罐。今天一句好話，明天一個小休息，年底你會被那個帳戶嚇到。
variations: 愛自己是定存，不是樂透。
別等一次愛夠，每天愛一點點就好。

en.content:
You don't have to love yourself all at once.
Save a little each day, and a year from now you'll find you've got some backbone.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's given up because 'I'm just bad at loving myself.' It's not a lump sum due today — it's a coin jar you drop a little into daily. One kind word today, one small rest tomorrow. By December that account will surprise you.
variations: Self-love is a savings plan, not a lottery ticket.
Don't wait to love yourself enough all at once. A little each day is plenty.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Adler 2026 healing quote '不完美才有人味，才是可愛之處'; rephrased into a warm conversational line

zh-tw.content:
那些你想藏起來的不完美，
其實正是別人覺得你很有人味的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種拼命想把自己磨成完美樣板的人。完全沒缺口的人其實有點難親近，你那些小毛邊、小笨拙，才是讓人想靠近的溫度。別急著磨平，那是你的可愛之處。
variations: 太完美的人沒有把手，別人不知道從哪裡靠近。
你的瑕疵不是扣分，是你之所以是你。

en.content:
The imperfections you want to hide
are exactly what make people feel you're human.
en.editorNote: For the person sanding themselves into a flawless template. People with zero rough edges are oddly hard to get close to — your little fumbles and awkward bits are the warmth that makes others want to lean in. Don't rush to smooth them out.
variations: Too-perfect people have no handles. No one knows where to grab on.
Your flaws aren't deductions. They're the reason you're you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-compassion practice 'forgive yourself, let go of past regret' theme; rephrased with concrete past-self framing

zh-tw.content:
你已經為以前那些事懲罰自己很久了。
是時候放她出來，一起過接下來的日子了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種半夜還在翻舊帳、為很久以前的某個決定自責的人。那件事你已經想過一千遍了，再想第一千零一遍也不會改變。原諒不是說它沒發生，是允許自己往前走。
variations: 你不是要忘記，是要停止用它打自己。
刑期到了，可以假釋自己了。

en.content:
You've punished yourself for the old stuff long enough.
It's time to let her out and live the days ahead together.
en.editorNote: For the person still relitigating ancient history at 2am, blaming themselves for a decision from years back. You've replayed it a thousand times — the thousand-and-first won't change it. Forgiving isn't pretending it didn't happen; it's letting yourself walk forward.
variations: You don't have to forget. You just have to stop hitting yourself with it.
You've served the sentence. You're allowed to grant yourself parole.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 viral IG caption 'your relationship with yourself sets the tone for every relationship'; rephrased into a conversational lifelong-relationship framing

zh-tw.content:
你這輩子最長的一段關係，是跟自己。
別把它經營成最糟的那一段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對朋友很好、對另一半很包容，卻天天嫌棄自己的人。從出生到最後，陪你最久的就是你自己。這段關係值得你用對待最在乎的人的方式去經營。
variations: 別人會來來去去，只有你會陪自己到最後。
你跟自己這對，沒有分手選項，那就好好相處吧。

en.content:
The longest relationship of your life is the one with yourself.
Don't let it become your worst one.
en.editorNote: For the person who's kind to friends and patient with partners but criticizes themselves daily. From birth to the very end, the one who's with you longest is you. That relationship deserves the same care you give the people you love most.
variations: People come and go. You're the one who stays till the end.
You and yourself? There's no breakup option. So make it a good one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased with conversational both-at-once framing

zh-tw.content:
你可以一邊接受現在的自己，
一邊慢慢變成更喜歡的樣子。
這兩件事不衝突。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「接受自己」就等於「放棄進步」的人。其實你可以先承認現在這個版本沒問題，再從這裡溫柔地往前走。先接住，再成長，順序別反了。
variations: 接受不是停在原地，是先站穩了再走。
喜歡現在的自己，跟想變更好，可以同時發生。

en.content:
You can accept who you are right now
and slowly grow into someone you like even more.
Those two don't cancel each other out.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'accepting yourself' means 'giving up on growth.' You can admit this version is fine and still walk forward from here, gently. Catch yourself first, then grow — don't flip the order.
variations: Acceptance isn't standing still. It's finding your footing before you move.
Liking who you are and wanting to be better can happen at the same time.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 賈靜雯 quote '愛自己的方式就是做你自己，面對並接受自己的好與不好'; rephrased into a conversational line

zh-tw.content:
愛自己最簡單的方式，
就是不再假裝自己是另一個人。
好的壞的，都一起認領。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種出門前要先想好「別人喜歡的版本」才敢做自己的人。你不用再花力氣演別人，把那些力氣留下來，好好當自己就好。連缺點都一起收編進來。
variations: 演別人很累，當自己最省力。
你的好跟壞是同一個人，不能只挑好的留。

en.content:
The simplest way to love yourself
is to stop pretending to be someone else.
The good and the bad — claim all of it.
en.editorNote: For the person who has to rehearse 'the version people like' before they dare to be themselves. You don't have to spend energy playing someone else. Save it, and just be you — flaws included.
variations: Playing someone else is exhausting. Being yourself takes the least effort.
Your good and your bad are the same person. You can't keep only the good half.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are worthy of kindness, your existence is a blessing' 2026 self-love roundup; rephrased without earning framing

zh-tw.content:
你不需要表現好，才值得被溫柔對待。
光是你願意撐到今天，就夠資格了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我要先做出成績，才配休息、才配被疼」的人。溫柔不是發給優等生的獎品，是你本來就該擁有的東西。今天有起床，就先給自己一份。
variations: 善待自己不用先及格，門檻只有「你還在」。
你不是靠表現換來被愛，你本來就值得。

en.content:
You don't have to perform well to deserve gentleness.
Making it to today is qualification enough.
en.editorNote: For the person who thinks 'I have to deliver results before I'm allowed to rest, or to be cared for.' Kindness isn't a prize for the top of the class — it's something you already get to have. You woke up today, so claim a little of it.
variations: Being kind to yourself has no passing grade. The only bar is: you're still here.
You don't earn love by performing. You were already worth it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 daily self-love roundup 'self-love is a marathon, not a sprint, with good days and bad days'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
愛自己這條路是馬拉松，不是百米衝刺。
有狀態好的日子，也會有走不動的日子。
重點不是跑多快，是別停下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種今天很愛自己、明天又掉回自我懷疑就覺得「我是不是退步了」的人。情緒本來就會起伏，今天倒退一點不代表前功盡棄。慢慢走也是在走。
variations: 退一步不算輸，停下來繼續呼吸就好。
今天愛不動自己沒關係，明天再接著愛。

en.content:
Loving yourself is a marathon, not a hundred-meter dash.
There'll be days you feel it, and days you can barely move.
The point isn't how fast you go — it's not stopping.
en.editorNote: For the person who loves themselves today, slides back into self-doubt tomorrow, and panics that they're 'regressing.' Feelings rise and fall. A bad day doesn't erase the progress. Walking slowly still counts as walking.
variations: A step back isn't a loss. Just keep breathing and keep going.
Can't muster the self-love today? Fine. Pick it back up tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 self-love roundup 'when you're gentle with yourself, the sharpness of the world softens'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
你對自己越溫柔，越扛得住世界的尖銳。
不是世界變軟了，是你終於不再幫它一起刺自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種被外面磨了一整天、回到家還繼續責備自己的人。世界硬歸硬，但你至少可以不要當那把最後一刀。對自己軟一點，反而更扛得住外面的硬。
variations: 對自己溫柔，不是逃避世界，是給自己留個能喘氣的地方。
外面已經夠刺了，你的內心可以是塊棉花。

en.content:
The gentler you are with yourself, the more of the world's sharpness you can take.
The world didn't get softer — you just stopped helping it stab you.
en.editorNote: For the person who gets ground down all day and then keeps scolding themselves at home. The world stays hard, but you don't have to be the last blade. Go soft on yourself, and you'll actually withstand more of the hard stuff outside.
variations: Being gentle with yourself isn't escaping the world. It's keeping one place where you can breathe.
It's sharp enough out there. Inside, you're allowed to be cotton.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing affirmation 'I give myself permission to heal' / 'I am healing gently'; rephrased with concrete pacing framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用一次就好起來。
慢慢好，也是好；好得歪歪扭扭，也算好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我都療傷這麼久了怎麼還沒好」而自責的人。復原沒有進度條，沒有及格線。今天比昨天好一點點，或只是沒更糟，都算數。別逼自己交報告。
variations: 傷口結痂的速度不歸你管，你只要不去摳它。
好起來不是比賽，沒有人在計時。

en.content:
You don't have to heal all at once.
Healing slowly still counts. Healing crooked still counts.
en.editorNote: For the person beating themselves up with 'I've been working on this so long, why am I not better yet?' Recovery has no progress bar, no passing grade. A little better than yesterday — or just not worse — counts. No report due.
variations: How fast a wound scabs over isn't up to you. Just don't pick at it.
Getting better isn't a race. No one's holding a stopwatch.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I deserve happiness and fulfillment' self-love affirmation; rephrased with anti-conditional framing

zh-tw.content:
你值得快樂，不附帶「等你瘦下來」、「等你升職」、「等你變更好」這些條件。
就現在這個版本，值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種把快樂設了一堆解鎖條件的人。幸福不是達標獎品，是你本來就可以拿的東西。別再把「值得」綁在還沒發生的事情上。
variations: 快樂不是達成 KPI 才能領的獎金。
你不用先變成誰，才有資格過得好。

en.content:
You deserve to be happy — no 'once I lose weight,' 'once I get promoted,' 'once I'm better' attached.
This version of you, right now, qualifies.
en.editorNote: For the person who's set a pile of unlock conditions on their own happiness. Joy isn't a prize for hitting targets — it's something you were always allowed to take. Stop tying 'deserve' to things that haven't happened yet.
variations: Happiness isn't a bonus you collect after hitting your KPIs.
You don't have to become someone else first to be allowed a good life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 '愛自己的100句正向心語' roundup, daily-detail self-care theme; rephrased into a single conversational line

zh-tw.content:
愛自己藏在很小的地方：
好好吃一頓飯、準時關燈睡覺、把那雙磨腳的鞋丟掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給覺得「愛自己」很玄、不知道從哪做起的人。它不是要你去靜修、去旅行，是今天就能做的小事——換掉用了三年的牙刷、喝夠水、不再委屈自己穿那雙會痛的鞋。
variations: 對自己好，從不再勉強自己用爛掉的東西開始。
愛自己有時候就是：餓了就吃，累了就睡，痛了就停。

en.content:
Self-love hides in tiny places:
eating a real meal, turning off the light on time, throwing out the shoes that hurt your feet.
en.editorNote: For anyone who finds 'self-love' too vague to start. It's not a retreat or a trip — it's the small stuff you can do today: replace the toothbrush you've had for three years, drink enough water, stop forcing yourself into shoes that ache.
variations: Being good to yourself starts with not making do with broken things.
Sometimes self-love is just: eat when hungry, sleep when tired, stop when it hurts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'it's not selfish to be gentle and kind to yourself, not a waste to heal from past patterns'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
花時間照顧自己的心，不是浪費時間。
你不是在原地打轉，你是在把以前沒處理完的事，慢慢收乾淨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我都幾歲了還在處理這些」而焦慮的人。整理內心不是退步，是把房間裡堆了很久的東西終於收一收。看起來沒在前進，其實是在把地基補好。
variations: 處理舊傷不是繞遠路，是把行李減輕再上路。
你不是卡住，你是在維修，維修完跑更快。

en.content:
Spending time caring for your own heart isn't a waste.
You're not stuck in place — you're quietly clearing up the things you never got to finish before.
en.editorNote: For the person anxiously thinking 'how am I still dealing with this at my age?' Tidying your inner world isn't regression — it's finally clearing out a room that's been piled up for years. Looks like no progress, but you're reinforcing the foundation.
variations: Working through old wounds isn't a detour. It's lightening your luggage before the road ahead.
You're not stuck. You're under maintenance — and you'll run faster after.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'I am my own safe place / I can rely on myself' self-love affirmation; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
你可以是自己最安全的地方。
外面再亂，至少回到自己身上的時候，你是不會凶自己的那個人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種在外面戴了一天面具、回家卻連對自己都不溫柔的人。家不一定是某個地方，可以是你對自己說話的語氣。先把那個地方整理成你願意待的樣子。
variations: 別人能不能接住你不一定，但你可以練習當自己的避風港。
全世界都對你大小聲時，至少你心裡那個聲音是站你這邊的。

en.content:
You can be your own safest place.
No matter how chaotic it gets out there, when you come back to yourself, you can be the one who doesn't snap at you.
en.editorNote: For the person who wears a mask all day and then isn't even gentle with themselves at home. Home isn't always a place — it can be the tone you use on yourself. Make that place somewhere you'd actually want to stay.
variations: Whether others catch you isn't guaranteed. But you can practice being your own shelter.
When the whole world is raising its voice at you, at least keep the one in your head on your side.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Buddha-style 2026 update line 'no one in the universe more deserving of your love than yourself'; rephrased with conversational framing

zh-tw.content:
找遍全世界，最該被你疼的那個人，
其實一直站在你照鏡子的那個位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種對誰都好、唯獨忘了對自己好的人。你花了那麼多力氣找一個值得愛的人，結果他一直在鏡子裡等你。今天先把那份愛分一點回來。
variations: 你一直在等的那個對的人，可能就是你自己。

en.content:
Search the whole world for the person most deserving of your love,
and you'll find them standing right where you look in the mirror.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's kind to everyone but forgets to be kind to themselves. You spent all that energy looking for someone worth loving — they've been waiting in the mirror the whole time. Send some of that love back home today.
variations: The right person you've been waiting for might just be you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 莫莉 2026 quote '最應該討好的人是自己'; rephrased with concrete confidence framing

zh-tw.content:
最該討好的人，是你自己。
把注意力轉回自己身上的那一秒，自信就自己跑出來了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一直在猜「別人喜不喜歡我」的朋友。與其花一整天討好整個世界，不如先把今天的好心情留給自己。你舒服了，氣場自然就對了。
variations: 別人的喜歡是甜點，自己的喜歡才是正餐。

en.content:
The person you should be winning over is yourself.
The second you turn the attention back to you, the confidence shows up on its own.
en.editorNote: For the friend always guessing whether other people like them. Instead of spending all day pleasing the world, save today's good mood for yourself first. When you're at ease, the rest falls into place.
variations: Other people's approval is dessert. Your own is the main course.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 劉冠吟《女子力不是溫柔，是戰鬥》 line 'you're worth a lifetime of your own company'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
可愛又可惡、獨一無二的你，
值得你花一輩子，好好陪自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得自己「很麻煩」、「很難搞」的朋友。你不需要先變成乖巧好相處的版本才值得被陪。連那些惱人的小毛病一起，都是你。這趟人生最長的伴，是你自己。
variations: 你身上那些你嫌棄的部分，也是陪你最久的部分。

en.content:
Lovable and infuriating, one of a kind —
you're worth a lifetime of your own company.
en.editorNote: For the friend who thinks they're 'too much' or 'high-maintenance.' You don't have to become the easy, agreeable version to deserve company. The annoying little quirks are you too. The longest companion of this life is yourself.
variations: The parts of you that you can't stand are also the parts that have stayed the longest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 Brit+Co affirmation 'I deserve to enjoy myself without guilt'; rephrased with Taiwanese conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
你值得開心，而且不用為這份開心道歉。
快樂不是偷來的，你不欠任何人一個解釋。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一放鬆就有罪惡感、玩得開心還要先擔心「是不是太爽了」的人。你不是來這世界贖罪的，開心本來就是你該有的份。理直氣壯地快樂吧。
variations: 快樂不用打卡請假，那是你本來就有的。

en.content:
You deserve to enjoy yourself, and you don't owe anyone an apology for it.
Happiness isn't stolen. You don't owe anyone an explanation.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels guilty the moment they relax and worries 'is this too good?' even while having fun. You didn't come here to atone — joy is your share by default. Be happy without flinching.
variations: Happiness doesn't need a permission slip. It was always yours.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
我無條件接受現在的自己，
同時，留給自己慢慢長大的空間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種以為「接受自己」就等於「放棄進步」的人。其實兩件事可以同時發生：現在的你已經夠好，未來的你還可以更好。接受不是終點，是讓你能好好出發的起跑線。
variations: 先接住現在的自己，才有力氣往前走。
接受自己不是擺爛，是給成長留呼吸的空間。

en.content:
I accept myself, exactly as I am right now —
and I leave myself room to keep growing.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'accepting yourself' means 'giving up on getting better.' Both can be true at once: today's you is already enough, and tomorrow's you can still grow. Acceptance isn't the finish line — it's the starting block that lets you actually move.
variations: Catch the version of you that exists now, and you'll have the strength to move forward.
Accepting yourself isn't slacking. It's leaving room for growth to breathe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 affirmation 'your rest is meaningful and necessary, resting is productive'; rephrased with Taiwanese conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，
是你還想好好走下去的證明。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一停下來就覺得「我是不是太廢」的人。手機沒電都要充，憑什麼你不行？休息不是進度條停下來，是你在偷偷存電，明天才走得遠。
variations: 電池都要充電了，你憑什麼一直放電？
今天躺平不是擺爛，是明天的伏筆。

en.content:
Resting isn't slacking.
It's proof you still intend to go the distance.
en.editorNote: For the person who feels lazy the moment they stop. Your phone gets to recharge — why don't you? Rest isn't the progress bar pausing; it's you quietly storing battery so tomorrow goes further.
variations: Even batteries recharge. Why are you running yourself flat?
Lying down today isn't quitting. It's the setup for tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by June 2026 weekly affirmation 'release comparison and return to self-love'; rephrased with concrete social-media framing

zh-tw.content:
別人的精彩是他的，
你的步調也是你的，沒有誰欠誰一個交代。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種滑完限動就開始懷疑人生的人。你看到的是別人剪輯過的精華，他沒拍的崩潰你也沒看到。把手機放下，回到自己的進度，那才是唯一真的。
variations: 你看到的是別人的精華片段，不是幕後 NG。
跟別人比，永遠輸在你只看得到他的高光。

en.content:
Their highlight reel is theirs.
Your pace is yours — neither of you owes the other an explanation.
en.editorNote: For the person who finishes a scroll through Stories and starts questioning their whole life. You're seeing someone's edited best-of — the breakdowns they didn't film, you didn't see either. Put the phone down and return to your own progress. That's the only real one.
variations: You're watching their highlight reel, not their bloopers.
Comparison always loses, because you only ever see their best take.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 viral IG caption 'plot twist: the person I was waiting for was me this whole time'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
劇情大反轉：
我一直在等的那個人，原來就是我自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合當 IG 限動或貼文文案，配一張自拍剛剛好。給那種一直等別人來救自己、來愛自己的人。等了那麼久，主角其實一直坐在鏡子前面。
variations: 等了半天的真命天子，原來是浴室鏡子裡那位。
本劇最大反派和最後的救世主，都是我自己。

en.content:
Plot twist:
the person I'd been waiting for was me the whole time.
en.editorNote: Perfect as an Instagram caption with a selfie. For anyone who keeps waiting for someone to come save them or love them. After all that waiting, the lead role was sitting in front of the mirror the entire time.
variations: Spent years waiting for The One. Turns out it's the one in the bathroom mirror.
The villain and the hero of this story? Both me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 healing affirmation 'don't rush the process, you don't need everything figured out'; rephrased with concrete framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用今天就把人生想清楚。
答案會在你願意慢下來的時候，自己浮出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種半夜想太多、覺得「再不搞懂自己要什麼就完了」的人。人生不是限時搶答，想不通的事先放著，它沒在追你。慢慢來，比較快。
variations: 想不通就先睡，腦袋關機重開常常就好了。
你不是落後，你只是還在醞釀。

en.content:
You don't have to figure out your whole life today.
The answers tend to surface once you're willing to slow down.
en.editorNote: For the person who overthinks at 2am, convinced they're doomed if they don't figure out what they want right now. Life isn't a speed round — leave the unsolved stuff alone, it isn't chasing you. Slow is often faster.
variations: Can't figure it out? Sleep on it. A reboot fixes more than you'd think.
You're not falling behind. You're still brewing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 funny self-love caption trend ('self love is my spirit animal, no filter required'); rephrased into original humorous line

zh-tw.content:
我對自己的喜歡，
是那種不用開美顏、不用等按讚的喜歡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 走幽默路線的自愛文案，當 IG 貼文超適合。重點是那種不靠外掛、不靠別人認證的自信。素顏也好、零讚也罷，你對自己的好評不下架。
variations: 別人按不按讚我不知道，反正我這票一定投我自己。
我這人最大的優點，就是很會欣賞我自己。

en.content:
The way I like myself
needs no filter and waits for no likes.
en.editorNote: A humorous take on self-love, great as an Instagram post. The point is confidence that doesn't run on plug-ins or outside approval. Bare face, zero likes — your own five-star review stays up regardless.
variations: Don't know if anyone else is liking this, but my vote's already on me.
My best quality? I'm extremely good at appreciating myself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'you were always worthy, worthiness was only forgotten not damaged' affirmation; rephrased with original framing

zh-tw.content:
你不是後來才變得值得，
你只是中間有一段，忘記自己一直都值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「等我變更好才配被愛」的人。你的價值沒有壞掉，也沒有打折，它只是被太多別人的標準蓋住了。今天不是重新賺回來，是把它擦乾淨而已。
variations: 你的價值不是新辦的會員卡，是出廠就內建的。
不是你變值得了，是你終於想起來了。

en.content:
You didn't become worthy later on.
There was just a stretch where you forgot you always were.
en.editorNote: For anyone who thinks 'I'll deserve love once I'm better.' Your worth isn't broken or on discount — it just got buried under everyone else's standards. Today isn't earning it back. It's wiping it clean.
variations: Your worth isn't a membership you signed up for. It came pre-installed.
You didn't become worthy. You just finally remembered.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'rest is joyful, being is joyful, my value isn't my productivity' affirmation; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
休息不是你做完事的獎勵，
是你身為一個人，本來就有的權利。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「我還沒做完，憑什麼休息」的工作狂朋友。你不是一台要先產出才能充電的機器。累了就停，不用先繳交成果證明。今天先躺平一下也沒人會扣你分。
variations: 你不用先值班滿八小時，才准對自己好。
休息不用申請，也不用核准。

en.content:
Rest isn't the prize for finishing your work.
It's a right you already have, just for being a person.
en.editorNote: For the workaholic friend who says 'I haven't finished — how can I rest?' You're not a machine that has to output before it's allowed to charge. Tired? Stop. No proof of productivity required. Lying down today won't dock your points.
variations: You don't have to clock a full shift before you're allowed to be kind to yourself.
Rest doesn't need an application. Or an approval.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 素黑 '自愛是承認你不是完人，別跟自己過不去' framing; rephrased into a single conversational line

zh-tw.content:
愛自己其中很重要的一件事，
是別再跟自己過不去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一件小事搞砸就能自責一整週的人。你已經夠累了，不需要再當那個追著自己罵的人。承認自己不是完人，不是認輸，是放過自己一馬。
variations: 你最該和好的人，是你自己。
別當自己最嚴格的考官，當自己最挺的隊友。

en.content:
One of the most important parts of loving yourself
is to stop giving yourself such a hard time.
en.editorNote: For the person who can beat themselves up for a week over one small slip. You're tired enough already — you don't need to be the one chasing yourself down with criticism. Admitting you're not flawless isn't giving up. It's cutting yourself some slack.
variations: The person you most need to make peace with is yourself.
Stop being your own strictest examiner. Be your own most loyal teammate.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'beneath comparison and self-doubt lies an intrinsic worth never damaged' theme; rephrased with anti-comparison angle

zh-tw.content:
你不是花，不用跟隔壁的開比快。
你按自己的季節長，就剛剛好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給滑完一輪 IG、看誰升遷誰結婚就開始焦慮的你。比較是把別人的時間表硬套在自己身上。你有你的節氣，別人春天開花的時候，你可能正在長根。
variations: 你不是慢，你只是有自己的時區。
別人的進度條不是你的進度條。

en.content:
You're not a flower racing the one next door to bloom.
You grow on your own season, and that's exactly right.
en.editorNote: For when you've scrolled past another promotion, another wedding, and the anxiety kicks in. Comparison is forcing someone else's timeline onto your own. You've got your own seasons — while others bloom in spring, you might be busy growing roots.
variations: You're not slow. You're just on your own time zone.
Someone else's progress bar isn't yours.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 2026 'I accept myself unconditionally and give myself grace to grow' affirmation; rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
我接受現在這個版本的自己，
同時也留一點空間，讓她慢慢變得更好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「接受自己」和「想進步」好像在打架的人。其實兩件事可以同時發生：你可以喜歡現在的自己，又願意往前走。接受不是停在原地，是帶著善意一起前進。
variations: 喜歡現在的自己，跟想變更好，從來不衝突。
我給自己的不是壓力，是一點長大的空間。

en.content:
I accept the version of me I am right now,
and still leave room for her to slowly become more.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels 'accepting myself' and 'wanting to grow' are at war. They aren't — both can happen at once. You can like who you are today and still be willing to move forward. Acceptance isn't standing still. It's walking on, with kindness in tow.
variations: Liking who you are now and wanting to grow were never in conflict.
What I'm giving myself isn't pressure. It's a little room to grow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Anna Taylor 'love yourself enough to set boundaries; you teach people how to treat you' theme; rephrased into Taiwan conversational tone

zh-tw.content:
你怎麼對待自己，就是在教別人怎麼對待你。
夠愛自己的人，才畫得出界線。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種怕拒絕別人、總是把自己的時間讓出去的朋友。畫界線不是兇，是讓對方知道你的時間和心力也很珍貴。你先把自己當一回事，別人才會跟著當一回事。
variations: 你的時間不是公共財，要留一點給自己。
說「不」不會讓你變壞人，只會讓你變成有界線的人。

en.content:
The way you treat yourself teaches everyone else how to treat you.
Only someone who loves themselves enough can draw a line.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's scared to say no and keeps giving their time away. A boundary isn't being mean — it's letting people know your time and energy matter too. Take yourself seriously first, and others will follow.
variations: Your time isn't public property. Keep some for yourself.
Saying no doesn't make you the bad guy. It makes you someone with boundaries.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'self-love is sometimes choosing to rest without guilt; you don't need to earn rest' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先賺到。
你光是累了，就已經夠資格停下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種「還沒做完不敢休息」的人。我們常以為休息是獎勵，要先夠努力才配得到。其實累了就是停下來的理由，不需要先交出什麼成績。
variations: 你不是機器，不用一直亮著綠燈。
停下來不是偷懶，是讓自己還能走得遠。

en.content:
You don't have to earn your rest.
Being tired is reason enough to stop.
en.editorNote: For the person who won't rest until everything's done. We treat rest like a reward you have to deserve — but being tired is already reason enough. You don't owe anyone a finished to-do list before you're allowed to breathe.
variations: You're not a machine. You don't have to stay switched on.
Stopping isn't slacking. It's how you keep going.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion affirmation 'I am listening to what I need in this moment'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
不用想清楚一整年要怎麼過。
先問問自己，此刻最需要的是什麼。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一焦慮就想把人生規劃到底的你。其實照顧自己沒那麼複雜，從「我現在需要什麼」開始就好——可能是一杯水、一通電話、或十分鐘什麼都不做。
variations: 照顧自己，從聽見自己現在的需要開始。
你不用解決全部，只要先回應眼前的這一件。

en.content:
You don't have to figure out the whole year.
Just ask yourself what you need most right now.
en.editorNote: For when anxiety makes you want to plan your entire life at once. Taking care of yourself isn't that complicated — start with 'what do I need right now?' Maybe it's a glass of water, a phone call, or ten minutes of doing nothing.
variations: Caring for yourself starts with hearing what you need right now.
You don't have to solve all of it. Just answer the thing in front of you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Maya Angelou 'you alone are enough, you have nothing to prove to anybody' theme; rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你不欠任何人一個「值得被愛」的證明。
你本來就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總覺得要更瘦、更成功、更厲害才配被愛的你。自我價值不是考試，不用一直交成績。你不是因為做到了什麼才值得，你存在本身就值得。
variations: 你不是達標才被愛，你是本來就被愛。
把那張「證明自己」的考卷收起來吧，你早就及格了。

en.content:
You don't owe anyone proof that you're worth loving.
You were already enough.
en.editorNote: For when you feel you have to be thinner, more successful, more impressive before you deserve love. Self-worth isn't an exam — you don't have to keep handing in scores. You're not worthy because you achieved something. You're worthy because you exist.
variations: You're not loved for hitting the target. You were loved all along.
Put down that 'prove yourself' exam. You passed a long time ago.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by boundaries-as-self-love theme (Anna Taylor / Brené Brown); rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
設界線不是把人推開，
是告訴自己：我的時間跟心力，值得被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一拒絕別人就內疚半天的你。畫界線不是變冷漠，是先把自己當一回事——別人怎麼對你，很多時候是你教會他們的。
variations: 會拒絕，是因為你終於開始把自己放進考量裡。
你不是難相處，你只是不再免費消耗自己。

en.content:
Setting a boundary isn't pushing people away.
It's telling yourself your time and energy deserve care.
en.editorNote: For when saying no leaves you feeling guilty for hours. A boundary isn't coldness — it's finally counting yourself in. A lot of the time, you teach people how to treat you.
variations: Learning to say no means you've started factoring yourself in.
You're not difficult. You just stopped giving yourself away for free.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are worthy right now, you don't have to earn it' affirmation; rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不用靠贏來的。
你現在這樣，就已經算數了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種總覺得要再厲害一點才配得上什麼的你。價值不是搶來的名次，也不用打敗誰。你不用做到什麼才被算進來——你本來就在。
variations: 不用贏過誰，你也一直都在名單上。
你的份量，不是比較出來的。

en.content:
Your worth isn't something you win.
The way you are right now already counts.
en.editorNote: For when you feel you have to be a little more impressive before you deserve anything. Worth isn't a ranking you fight for, and you don't have to beat anyone for it. You don't have to achieve your way in — you're already here.
variations: You don't have to outdo anyone. You were always on the list.
Your value was never measured by comparison.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-acceptance / stop comparing aesthetic quotes; rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你不是別人的比較版本，
你是原版，本來就沒得比。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一滑手機就開始覺得自己不夠好的你。別人的精彩是別人的，你有你自己的節奏跟樣子。跟自己比就好——今天比昨天多喜歡自己一點，就贏了。
variations: 你的人生不用對照別人的進度表。
把眼光收回來，你身上早就有值得看的東西。

en.content:
You're not a lesser copy of someone else.
You're the original — there's nothing to compare.
en.editorNote: For when scrolling your phone starts making you feel not-good-enough. Other people's highlights are theirs; you have your own pace and shape. Only compare yourself to yourself — if you like yourself a little more today than yesterday, you've already won.
variations: Your life doesn't have to run on someone else's timeline.
Bring your eyes back to yourself. There's already plenty worth seeing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion research on treating setbacks gently; rephrased into conversational Taiwan tone

zh-tw.content:
你今天沒做到的事，
不代表你這個人有問題。
只是今天的你，累了而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一天結束沒把待辦事項清完，就開始自我檢討的你。人不是機器，有電量也有低潮。把「我今天很爛」換成「我今天累了」，語氣一換，心就鬆一半。
variations: 不是你不夠好，是你今天需要休息。
做不完的清單明天還在，但你要先撐住自己。

en.content:
The thing you didn't get to today
doesn't mean something's wrong with you.
It just means today's version of you was tired.
en.editorNote: For when you end the day with an unfinished to-do list and immediately start beating yourself up. You're not a machine — you have battery levels and off days too. Swap 'I was terrible today' for 'I was tired today.' Same day, gentler story.
variations: It's not that you're not enough. You just needed rest today.
The list will still be there tomorrow. Get yourself through tonight first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by boundary-setting as self-respect; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
會因為你設界線就離開的人，
本來就不是為了愛你而留下的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一想拒絕別人就內疚半天的你。說「不」不會趕走真正在乎你的人，只會篩掉一直在消耗你的人。你的界線不是圍牆，是門——決定誰能進來的，是你。
variations: 你的「不」，是在保護你的「好」。
留下來的人，是接受完整的你，包括你的底線。

en.content:
Anyone who leaves the moment you set a boundary
was never really staying to love you.
en.editorNote: For when saying no makes you feel guilty for hours. A 'no' won't push away the people who actually care — it just filters out the ones who kept draining you. Your boundaries aren't a wall, they're a door. You decide who comes in.
variations: Your 'no' is protecting your 'yes.'
The ones who stay accept the whole you — limits included.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you don't have to feel it to start doing it' self-love theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你不用先愛上自己，才開始好好對自己。
有時候是先好好對自己，愛才慢慢長出來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種覺得「我根本不喜歡自己，怎麼可能自愛」而卡住的你。自愛不是一種要先有的心情，而是一件件小事：好好吃飯、按時睡覺、不再罵自己。做著做著，感覺才會跟上來。
variations: 先照顧，再談喜歡，順序反過來也可以。
愛自己是動詞，不是等到準備好才做的事。

en.content:
You don't have to love yourself first before you treat yourself well.
Sometimes you treat yourself well first, and the love grows in later.
en.editorNote: For when you're stuck thinking 'I don't even like myself, how am I supposed to love myself?' Self-love isn't a feeling you need upfront — it's a stack of small acts: eating properly, sleeping on time, stopping the self-insults. Do them long enough and the feeling catches up.
variations: Care first, liking later. The order can run backwards.
Loving yourself is a verb, not something you wait to feel ready for.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by radical self-acceptance and inner-critic themes; rephrased into casual tone

zh-tw.content:
你腦中那個一直挑你毛病的聲音，
不是真相，只是一個很吵的習慣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一犯錯，腦內小劇場就開始無限循環罵自己的你。那個聲音講得再理直氣壯，也不代表它說的是對的。習慣是可以改的——每次它一出現，就練習回一句「謝謝提醒，但這次我選擇對自己好一點」。
variations: 會罵你的聲音很大，不代表它最有道理。
你不是那個內心的批評家，你是聽見它、也能選擇不信它的人。

en.content:
That voice in your head that keeps picking you apart
isn't the truth. It's just a really loud habit.
en.editorNote: For when one mistake sends your inner monologue into an endless loop of self-criticism. However confident that voice sounds, that doesn't make it right. Habits can be rewired — each time it shows up, practice answering: 'Thanks for the note, but this time I'm choosing to be kinder to myself.'
variations: The voice that scolds you is loud. That doesn't make it correct.
You're not the inner critic. You're the one who hears it and gets to not believe it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'worth is not conditional on productivity' theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你的價值，不是靠今天做了多少事來計算的。
就算你整天什麼都沒做，你還是值得被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一放假耍廢就開始有罪惡感的你。你的存在本身就有價值，不是一張要靠生產力去換分數的成績單。休息不是偷懶，是你身為人本來就有的權利。
variations: 你不是一台要證明自己有用的機器。
沒有產出的一天，你依然完整，依然值得。

en.content:
Your worth isn't calculated by how much you got done today.
Even on a day you did nothing at all, you still deserve to be treated well.
en.editorNote: For when a lazy day off immediately triggers guilt. Your existence has value on its own — it's not a report card you have to earn with productivity. Rest isn't slacking; it's a basic right of being a person.
variations: You're not a machine that has to prove it's useful.
On a day with zero output, you're still whole, still worthy.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'protecting my peace' boundaries theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
劃界線不是把人推開，
是告訴自己：我的平靜也值得被保護。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種一拒絕別人就會愧疚到睡不著的你。界線不是拒人於千里，而是幫關係留一個彼此都舒服的距離。你可以善良，也可以有底線，這兩件事不衝突。
variations: 說「不」的時候，其實是在對自己說「好」。
你不欠任何人一個沒有界線的自己。

en.content:
Setting a boundary isn't pushing people away.
It's telling yourself: my peace deserves protecting too.
en.editorNote: For anyone who feels so guilty after saying no that they can't sleep. Boundaries don't shut people out — they keep a comfortable distance so the relationship can breathe. You can be kind and still have limits; the two don't cancel out.
variations: Every time you say no to them, you're saying yes to you.
You don't owe anyone a version of yourself with no limits.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'stop apologizing for who you are' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
別再為了自己的存在道歉。
你不用縮小，才有位子留給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種連在群組裡發言都要先打「不好意思」的你。你不是來借空間的客人，你本來就有資格佔一個位子。下次想道歉之前，先確認：我真的做錯了，還是只是太習慣讓自己變小？
variations: 你不是來借過的，你本來就在這裡。
把「不好意思」換成「謝謝你」，你會發現自己沒有錯。

en.content:
Stop apologizing for taking up space.
You don't have to shrink to deserve a seat.
en.editorNote: For the person who types 'sorry' before every message in the group chat. You're not a guest borrowing room — you have a right to be here. Next time you reach for 'sorry,' check: did I actually do something wrong, or am I just used to making myself small?
variations: You're not squeezing past. You already belong here.
Swap 'sorry' for 'thank you' and notice you did nothing wrong.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Kristin Neff self-compassion vs self-criticism framing; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
自我批評問的是「你夠好了嗎」，
自我疼惜問的是「你現在需要什麼」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種只要一犯錯就在心裡罵自己半天的你。罵自己不會讓你變更好，只會讓傷口更深。下次卡住的時候，別再審問自己，改問一句：我現在需要的是什麼？答案通常是一杯水、一場覺，或一句沒關係。
variations: 你不需要罵醒自己，你需要的是有人陪你撐過去。
對自己溫柔，不是放縱，是修復。

en.content:
Self-criticism asks, 'Are you good enough yet?'
Self-compassion asks, 'What do you need right now?'
en.editorNote: For the person who mentally scolds themselves for an hour over one small mistake. Beating yourself up won't make you better — it just digs the wound deeper. Next time you're stuck, stop the interrogation and ask instead: what do I actually need right now? Usually it's water, sleep, or a quiet 'it's okay.'
variations: You don't need to yell yourself awake. You need someone to sit with you.
Being gentle with yourself isn't indulgence — it's repair.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by inner-child healing theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
小時候沒人替你說的那句「你已經很棒了」，
現在你可以自己說給自己聽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種長大後還在追別人肯定的你。有些等待了很久的話，其實不必再等別人開口。你現在有能力，當那個小時候你最需要的大人。試著對心裡那個小孩說一句：辛苦了，我看到你了。
variations: 你追了很久的那句肯定，其實你早就有資格自己給。
當那個小時候的你最需要的大人，永遠不嫌晚。

en.content:
The 'you did great' no one said to you as a kid —
you get to say it to yourself now.
en.editorNote: For anyone who grew up still chasing other people's approval. Some words you've waited a long time for, you don't have to wait for anymore. You're now able to be the grown-up your younger self needed most. Try telling that kid inside: that was hard, and I see you.
variations: The approval you've chased for years — you were always allowed to give it yourself.
It's never too late to be the adult your younger self needed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are enough, not too much' affirmation theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你不是太多，
只是遇到了裝不下你的容器。
zh-tw.editorNote: 給那種常被說「你想太多」「你太敏感」而開始懷疑自己的人。你的深度、你的在乎，都不是缺點，只是有些人剛好接不住。別為了配合小杯子，把自己倒掉一半。對的人，會剛好喜歡完整的你。
variations: 不是你太滿，是那個杯子太小。
留在會嫌你太多的地方，才是真的委屈了自己。

en.content:
You're not too much.
You just met a container too small to hold you.
en.editorNote: For anyone told 'you overthink' or 'you're too sensitive' until they started doubting themselves. Your depth, your caring — those aren't flaws; some people just can't hold them. Don't pour half of yourself out to fit a small cup. The right people will love the whole of you exactly as you are.
variations: It's not that you're too full — the cup was just too small.
Staying where you're called 'too much' is the real waste of you.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 你不需要把自己縮到最小，才配得上一個位置。
zh-tw.editorNote: 提醒自己不必為了討好而委屈，適合設立界線、拒絕過度配合時使用。
variations: 你不必變得更小，才有資格被留下。
縮小自己換來的空間，從來都不是真正屬於你的。

en.content: You don't have to shrink yourself to earn a place at the table.
en.editorNote: For moments when you're tempted to play small just to keep the peace.
variations: Making yourself smaller was never the price of belonging.
You deserve room to take up space, not just permission to exist quietly.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 對自己溫柔一點，你已經撐過了那些以為撐不過的日子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在低潮、自責或疲憊時，給自己一點肯定與喘息。
variations: 別忘了誇誇自己，那些難熬的日子你都走過來了。
你比自己想的還要堅強，只是你太習慣忽略這件事。

en.content: Be a little gentler with yourself; you've survived every day you thought you couldn't.
en.editorNote: For rough days when you need a reminder of how much you've already made it through.
variations: Give yourself some credit; you outlasted the days that nearly broke you.
You're tougher than you give yourself credit for, you just forget to notice.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content: 喜歡自己不是等到變完美的那天，而是願意在不完美裡繼續愛著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合放下對完美的執著、練習接納當下的自己時使用。
variations: 愛自己不是抵達終點，而是一路上都選擇不放棄自己。
你不用先變好，才值得被自己好好對待。

en.content: Loving yourself isn't waiting until you're perfect; it's choosing to stay kind through the messy parts.
en.editorNote: For letting go of the 'I'll like myself once I fix everything' trap.
variations: Self-love isn't a finish line, it's choosing not to give up on you along the way.
You don't have to earn your own kindness by becoming flawless first.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by boundary-as-self-respect themes; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
每一次替自己畫下界線，
都是在小聲對自己說：你值得被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那個總是不好意思拒絕別人的朋友，或提醒剛學著說「不」的自己。設界線常伴隨罪惡感，這句話幫你把它翻譯成自我照顧。
variations: 拒絕別人不代表你壞，只代表你終於把自己放進考量。
界線不是把人推開，是把自己接回來。

en.content: Every boundary you set is a quiet way of telling yourself: I'm worth protecting.
en.editorNote: For a friend who can never say no, or for yourself when a new boundary still feels uncomfortable. Reframes guilt as self-care.
variations: Saying no doesn't make you cold. It just means you finally counted yourself in.
A boundary isn't pushing people away. It's pulling yourself back.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rest without guilt' affirmation theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你不用先累到不行，才有資格休息。
休息，本來就是你該擁有的，不是獎品。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一停下來就有罪惡感的工作狂朋友，或連休假都在焦慮的自己。很適合週五晚上發限動。
variations: 休息不是偷懶，是替明天的自己充電。
把腳步慢下來，不代表你落後，只代表你在照顧自己。

en.content:
You don't have to run yourself into the ground before you're allowed to rest.
Rest isn't a reward. It was always yours to take.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels guilty the moment they stop, or for yourself when even a day off makes you anxious. A good Friday-night Story.
variations: Resting isn't slacking off. It's recharging the person who has to show up tomorrow.
Slowing down doesn't mean you're falling behind. It means you're taking care of you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'affirmations that don't feel like a fraud' angle; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
如果「我超愛我自己」還說不出口，
那就先從「我願意對自己好一點」開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那些覺得肉麻自我肯定很假的人。自愛不用一步到位，這句話給的是一個做得到的起點。
variations: 還沒辦法愛自己沒關係，先別再罵自己就好。
從討厭到喜歡，中間有一站叫「還可以」，先在那裡歇一下。

en.content:
If "I love myself" still feels fake to say,
start with "I'm willing to be a little kinder to myself."
en.editorNote: For people who find gushy affirmations cringey. Self-love doesn't have to happen all at once — this gives a doable starting point.
variations: You don't have to love yourself yet. Just stop being your own worst critic for today.
Between hating yourself and loving yourself, there's a stop called 'good enough.' Rest there a while.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'protecting my peace' self-love theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
今年的主題很簡單：
保護我的平靜。
會讓我心累的人事物，先請它們排隊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合在人際關係斷捨離、或想遠離內耗時發。語氣半認真半幽默，很適合貼在 IG。
variations: 我的平靜不對外開放，謝謝配合。
會消耗我的東西，我開始學會不接電話。

en.content:
This year's whole theme is simple:
protect my peace.
Anything that drains me can go wait in line.
en.editorNote: For decluttering your relationships or stepping away from things that exhaust you. Half-serious, half-playful — great for Instagram.
variations: My peace is not open to the public. Thanks for understanding.
The things that drain me? I've started letting those calls go to voicemail.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion affirmation collection; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你對朋友那麼溫柔，
下次犯錯的時候，也把那份溫柔留一點給自己吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種對別人超好、對自己超嚴格的朋友。提醒對象包含每一個常常自責的你我。
variations: 你不會對摔倒的朋友大吼，那也別對自己這樣。
把你安慰別人的那套話，錄音起來，留給搞砸的自己聽。

en.content:
You're so gentle with your friends.
Next time you mess up, save a little of that gentleness for yourself.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's endlessly kind to others but brutal on themselves — which is most of us. A reminder for anyone stuck in self-blame.
variations: You'd never yell at a friend who tripped. So don't do it to yourself.
Whatever you'd say to comfort someone else — record it and play it back for yourself on the bad days.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by boundaries-as-devotion quote theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你不是被打造來縮小自己的，
是被打造來把自己活得更完整的。
別為了塞進不合的地方，把自己折起來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種老是在別人面前縮著、怕自己太多太亮的朋友。也適合在你剛拒絕一件會委屈自己的事之後，貼在 IG 給自己打氣。
variations: 你已經長大到裝不進那個舊房間了，這不是你的錯。
與其把自己削小去合群，不如去找容得下你的地方。

en.content:
You weren't built to shrink into less.
You were built to grow into more.
Stop folding yourself up to fit places you've outgrown.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps making themselves small so they won't be 'too much.' Also good to post after you've just said no to something that would've cost you yourself.
variations: You've grown too big for that old room. That's not a flaw.
Don't file yourself down to fit in. Go find the place that has room for you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my worth isn't based on my productivity' affirmation; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不是用今天做了多少事來算的。
你什麼都沒做的那天，也一樣值得被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一休息就有罪惡感的工作狂朋友，或整天躺著卻一直自責的自己。放假日、生病日拿出來讀特別有效。
variations: 你不是一台機器，不用一直有產出才有存在的理由。
休息不是偷懶，是你也需要被自己照顧。

en.content:
Your worth isn't measured by how much you got done today.
On the day you do nothing at all, you're still worth taking care of.
en.editorNote: For the workaholic friend who feels guilty the second they rest, or for yourself on a day spent horizontal and self-scolding. Hits hardest on a day off or a sick day.
variations: You're not a machine. You don't need constant output to justify existing.
Resting isn't slacking. You need looking after too — even from yourself.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I choose me' self-acceptance quote theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
當全世界都希望你選他們的時候，
有勇氣說出「這次我選我自己」，
就是自愛的開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給正卡在「幫別人」跟「顧自己」之間為難的朋友。也適合你剛做完一個讓別人失望、但對自己誠實的決定之後。
variations: 偶爾讓別人失望，總比一直讓自己失望好。
把自己放進待辦清單的第一項，不叫自私，叫必要。

en.content:
When the whole world expects you to choose them,
having the courage to say "this time, I choose me" —
that's where self-love begins.
en.editorNote: For the friend torn between helping everyone and keeping something for themselves. Also good right after you've made a choice that disappointed someone but was honest to you.
variations: Disappointing others once in a while beats disappointing yourself every day.
Putting yourself first on the to-do list isn't selfish. It's necessary.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you are enough as you are' quote theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你不用先變得更瘦、更成功、更完美，
才夠格喜歡自己。
你現在這個樣子，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種永遠在等「等我變更好再說」的朋友。也很適合照鏡子時對自己講一遍，當每天的提醒語。
variations: 「夠好」不是一個要達到的門檻，你早就在門內了。
別再把喜歡自己這件事，設成一個永遠達不到的條件。

en.content:
You don't have to get thinner, more successful, more perfect first
to be allowed to like yourself.
The way you are right now is already enough.
