en.editorNote: For the friend who's forever waiting until they're 'better' to love themselves. Also a good line to say to the mirror as a daily reminder.
variations: 'Good enough' isn't a bar to clear. You've been past it this whole time.
Stop setting your own approval behind a condition you can never quite meet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you teach people how to treat you' boundaries theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
別人怎麼對你，其實是你一次次默許出來的。
你願意接受什麼、拒絕什麼，
就是在教他們該怎麼對待你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一直忍、忍到自己都快不見的朋友。也適合你剛下定決心「這種對待我不再接受了」的那一刻，貼在筆記本第一頁提醒自己。
variations: 你的沉默，有時候會被當成同意。
設界線不是把人推開，是告訴對方你值得被好好對待。

en.content:
How people treat you is something you allow, one time at a time.
What you accept and what you refuse
is how you teach them to handle you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps putting up with things until they almost disappear. Also good the moment you decide 'I won't accept being treated like this anymore' — jot it on the first page of your notebook.
variations: Your silence sometimes gets read as a yes.
Setting a boundary isn't pushing people away. It's telling them you deserve to be treated well.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'comparison suffocates self-love' theme (Iyanla Vanzant); rephrased

zh-tw.content:
每次拿自己跟別人比，
你都在偷偷傷害那個努力活著的自己。
你的價值，不是靠贏過誰換來的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一滑手機就開始焦慮、覺得自己不夠好的朋友。也適合你又不小心跌進比較地獄的深夜，拿來把自己撈回來。
variations: 別人的精彩，不會讓你的人生打折。
你不是活在排行榜上，不用一直看名次。

en.content:
Every time you measure yourself against someone else,
you quietly wound the you that's out here trying.
Your worth was never won by beating anyone.
en.editorNote: For the friend who opens their phone and instantly feels not-enough. Also good on the late nights you slip back into the comparison pit — use it to pull yourself out.
variations: Someone else shining doesn't put your life on discount.
You're not living on a leaderboard. You don't have to keep checking your rank.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rest is productive, self-love isn't laziness' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
休息不是偷懶，是幫自己充電。
允許自己慢下來，
不代表你不夠努力，是你懂得心疼自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種累到不行、卻還在為「休息會不會太廢」而愧疚的朋友。也很適合你請假在家的那天，貼給自己一個許可。
variations: 會照顧自己的人，才走得久。
你不是機器，本來就不用一直運轉。

en.content:
Rest isn't slacking off — it's recharging.
Letting yourself slow down
doesn't mean you're lazy. It means you're kind enough to spare yourself.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's running on empty but still feels guilty that resting is 'lazy.' Also perfect for the day you take off — give yourself permission with it.
variations: The people who last are the ones who know how to take care of themselves.
You're not a machine. You were never meant to just keep running.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'be afraid of losing yourself trying to please everyone' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
別太害怕失去別人，
真正該怕的，
是為了討好每一個人，最後把自己搞丟了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種永遠在配合別人、卻越來越不像自己的朋友。也適合你剛拒絕了一個不想答應的請求、心裡還在打鼓的時候。
variations: 你不可能讓所有人都滿意，那就先讓自己滿意吧。
配合到失去自己，不叫善良，叫委屈。

en.content:
Don't be so scared of losing other people.
What should really scare you
is losing yourself while trying to please everyone.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's always accommodating others and slowly feeling less like themselves. Also good right after you've said no to something you didn't want, while your heart's still pounding.
variations: You can't keep everyone happy, so start with yourself.
Bending until you disappear isn't kindness. It's just self-neglect.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your value is inherent, nothing makes you worthy' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
沒有什麼事，是你「做到了」才配被愛的。
你的價值，不是靠表現換來的，
它本來就長在你身上。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種總覺得「等我做到某件事，才有資格被喜歡」的朋友。也適合你剛搞砸一件事、開始否定自己的時候，提醒你價值不會因此蒸發。
variations: 你不用先證明什麼，才值得被溫柔對待。
被愛不是獎品，不用先達標才能領。

en.content:
There's nothing you have to 'achieve' first to deserve love.
Your worth was never traded for performance —
it's been part of you all along.
en.editorNote: For the friend who thinks 'once I pull off this one thing, then I'll be worth liking.' Also good right after you've messed something up and started tearing yourself down — a reminder that your worth didn't evaporate.
variations: You don't have to prove anything to deserve gentleness.
Being loved isn't a prize. You don't have to hit a target to claim it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you teach people how to treat you by what you accept' boundary theme; fully rephrased

zh-tw.content:
設界線不是把人推開，
是告訴自己：我的時間跟力氣，也值得被好好對待。
別人怎麼對你，其實是你先默許的。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種老是不好意思拒絕別人、最後把自己累垮的朋友。也適合你剛學會說「不」、卻還在自我懷疑的時候，提醒你這不是自私。
variations: 會說「不」的人，不是難搞，是終於開始心疼自己。
你允許什麼，別人就會一直做什麼。

en.content:
Setting a boundary isn't pushing people away.
It's telling yourself: my time and energy deserve care too.
How people treat you starts with what you quietly allow.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can never bring themselves to say no and ends up burnt out. Also good for yourself right after you've finally set a limit but are still second-guessing it — a reminder that it isn't selfish.
variations: People who say 'no' aren't difficult. They just finally started caring for themselves.
Whatever you allow is what keeps happening.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Brene Brown 'talk to yourself as you would someone you love'; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你對自己講話的方式，
如果拿去對朋友講，早就沒朋友了。
試著用你安慰別人的那種語氣，安慰自己一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種對自己特別嚴苛、動不動就罵自己笨的朋友。也適合你今天又在心裡把自己數落一頓的時候，換個口氣重來一遍。
variations: 你對別人那麼溫柔，也留一點給自己好嗎？
腦裡那個一直挑你毛病的聲音，你可以叫它閉嘴。

en.content:
The way you talk to yourself —
if you said it to a friend, you'd have no friends left.
Try comforting yourself in the same voice you'd use on them.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's brutally hard on themselves and calls themselves stupid over nothing. Also good when you've just spent the day trashing yourself in your head — a nudge to run it back in a kinder tone.
variations: You're so gentle with everyone else. Save a little of that for you.
That nitpicking voice in your head? You're allowed to tell it to shut up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Robert Holden 'no self-improvement makes up for lack of self-acceptance'; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
再怎麼進步、再怎麼變好，
都補不了「打從心底不接受自己」這個洞。
先學會喜歡現在的你，改變才會是加分，不是贖罪。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一直在追「更好的自己」、卻永遠覺得不夠的朋友。也適合你剛立完一堆新計畫、又開始嫌棄現在的自己時，先喘口氣。
variations: 不接受自己的話，變再好都還是覺得少一塊。
成長是為了對自己更好，不是為了懲罰現在的你。

en.content:
No amount of leveling up
can fill the hole of not accepting yourself underneath.
Like who you are now first — then change becomes a bonus, not a penance.
en.editorNote: For the friend forever chasing a 'better self' who still never feels like enough. Also good right after you've set a pile of new goals and started resenting your current self — take a breath first.
variations: Without self-acceptance, even getting better still feels like something's missing.
Growth is meant to be kind to you, not a punishment for who you are now.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'your value is inherent, not earned by productivity' self-worth theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先賺到。
你不是機器，沒有「產能」達標才准喘氣這回事。
累了就停，這件事本身就夠正當了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一放假就有罪惡感、非得做點什麼才安心的朋友。也適合你躺著滑手機、卻一直被「該起來做正事」念頭追著跑的時候。
variations: 你不是靠有沒有用來決定值不值得休息的。
允許自己什麼都不做，也是一種對自己好。

en.content:
You don't have to earn rest.
You're not a machine that only gets to breathe once it hits a quota.
Being tired is reason enough to stop.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels guilty the moment they take a day off and has to be doing something to feel okay. Also good when you're lying down scrolling but keep getting chased by the 'get up and be productive' voice.
variations: Your worth isn't measured by how useful you were today.
Letting yourself do nothing is a way of being kind to yourself too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'stop comparing, run your own race' self-love theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
別人開得快，不代表你走錯路。
每個人的時區本來就不一樣，
你按自己的步調前進，也算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一打開 IG 看到同齡人又升遷又結婚，就開始否定自己的朋友。也適合你覺得「大家都超前、只有我卡住」的深夜。
variations: 你不是慢，你只是走在自己的時間裡。
花有花的花期，你急著開，反而開不好。

en.content:
Someone moving faster doesn't mean you took a wrong turn.
Everyone runs on a different clock —
moving at your own pace still counts.
en.editorNote: For the friend who opens Instagram, sees peers getting promoted and married, and instantly starts doubting themselves. Also good for those late nights when it feels like everyone's ahead and you're the only one stuck.
variations: You're not behind. You're just on your own timeline.
Every flower has its season. Rush the bloom and it won't come out right.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'love yourself enough to set boundaries' theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
會讓你有罪惡感的界線，通常正是你最需要的那一條。
拒絕別人不代表你壞，
只代表你終於把自己也算進去了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種永遠不敢說「不」、怕拒絕別人就會被討厭的朋友。也適合你剛拒絕完一個過分的請求、正在心虛的那五分鐘。
variations: 你不是變冷漠了，你只是不再免費消耗自己。
設界線會不舒服，是因為你以前太習慣委屈。

en.content:
The boundary that makes you feel guilty is usually the one you needed most.
Saying no doesn't make you a bad person —
it just means you finally counted yourself in too.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can never say no and is scared that turning someone down means being disliked. Also good for the guilty five minutes right after you've declined an unreasonable request.
variations: You didn't get colder. You just stopped running on empty for free.
Boundaries feel uncomfortable because you were too used to shrinking.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'you don't have to earn rest' / 'worth not tied to productivity' affirmations; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先賺來。
你不是機器，累了就停，
不是因為做完了什麼，只是因為你是人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一放假就焦慮、覺得沒生產力就沒價值的工作狂朋友。也適合你躺在沙發上卻一直想著「我是不是該做點正事」的週末午後。
variations: 你的價值不是用產出來計算的。
先休息，罪惡感等一下再說。

en.content:
You don't have to earn rest.
You're not a machine — you stop because you're tired,
not because you finished something, just because you're human.
en.editorNote: For the workaholic friend who gets anxious the moment they have a day off and feels worthless without being productive. Also for the weekend afternoon when you're lying on the couch but can't stop thinking you should be doing something 'real'.
variations: Your worth isn't measured in output.
Rest first. The guilt can wait.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'give yourself the compassion you give others' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你把最溫柔的話都留給別人，
卻用最狠的話對自己。
今天開始，也分一點溫柔給自己吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種對朋友超好、對自己超嚴格的人。也適合你又在半夜檢討自己、把一整天的小失誤放大成人格缺陷的時候。
variations: 你對別人那麼寬容，也值得對自己寬容一次。
如果朋友犯這個錯，你會這樣罵他嗎？不會，那就別罵自己。

en.content:
You save your gentlest words for everyone else,
then use the cruelest ones on yourself.
Starting today, spare a little of that kindness for you too.
en.editorNote: For the person who's endlessly kind to friends but brutally hard on themselves. Also for those nights when you replay every small slip-up and blow it up into a character flaw.
variations: You're so forgiving with others. You deserve to be forgiven once too.
If a friend made this mistake, would you talk to them like that? No — so don't talk to yourself like that.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'protecting my peace' self-love theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
保護自己的平靜，不需要跟任何人解釋。
有些人你不用討厭，
只是不用再讓他們住進你腦子裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種被某段關係反覆消耗、卻還在自責「是不是我太小題大作」的朋友。也適合你決定不再回某個訊息、想斷開某段內耗的那一刻。
variations: 你可以祝他好，然後轉身走遠。
把腦子的房間空出來，只留給讓你安心的人。

en.content:
Protecting your peace doesn't require anyone's explanation.
Some people you don't have to hate —
you just stop letting them live rent-free in your head.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps getting drained by one relationship yet still blames themselves for 'overreacting'. Also for the moment you decide not to reply to a certain text and cut loose from something that's been eating at you.
variations: You can wish them well and still walk away.
Clear out the rooms in your head. Keep them for the people who make you feel safe.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'rest without guilt' self-love theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
休息不用先把事情做完才配得到。
累了就先躺平，
世界不會因為你停下來就崩塌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一放假就有罪惡感、覺得「沒生產力就是在浪費生命」的朋友。也適合你明明累爆了，卻還在硬撐、逼自己再多做一點的那個下午。
variations: 你不是機器，不用 24 小時都在運轉。
先照顧好自己，待辦清單明天還會在那裡等你。

en.content:
You don't have to finish everything before you're allowed to rest.
Tired? Lie down first.
The world won't fall apart just because you paused.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels guilty the moment they take a day off, like being unproductive is wasting their life. Also for the afternoon when you're completely wiped but keep pushing yourself to do just a little more.
variations: You're not a machine. You don't have to run 24/7.
Take care of yourself first — the to-do list will still be there tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'worthiness isn't earned' self-love theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
別再等自己「夠好」才願意愛自己。
你的價值不是靠成績、身材或成就換來的，
它從你出生那天就在了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種老是覺得「等我瘦下來、等我升職、等我變厲害再說」的朋友。也適合你又在拿自己跟別人比、覺得現在的自己不值得被善待的時候。
variations: 你不用先變厲害，才有資格喜歡自己。
價值不是考出來的，是你本來就有的。

en.content:
Stop waiting until you're 'good enough' to love yourself.
Your worth isn't traded for grades, a body, or achievements —
it's been there since the day you were born.
en.editorNote: For the friend stuck in 'once I lose the weight, once I get promoted, once I'm better' mode. Also for when you're comparing yourself to others and feel like the current you doesn't deserve any kindness.
variations: You don't have to become impressive first to be allowed to like yourself.
Worth isn't something you test into. You already have it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by self-compassion affirmations for hard days; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
日子很難的時候，對自己溫柔一點。
你不用假裝沒事，
光是撐到現在，就已經很努力了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給正在低潮、什麼都提不起勁的朋友。也適合你今天只是「活著」就用盡全力、卻還在怪自己不夠積極的那種日子。
variations: 撐到今天的你，已經很勇敢了。
不用馬上好起來，慢慢來也可以。

en.content:
On the hard days, be gentle with yourself.
You don't have to pretend you're fine —
just making it this far already took a lot.
en.editorNote: For a friend in a low patch who can't muster energy for anything. Also for the kind of day when simply staying alive drained everything you had, yet you're still scolding yourself for not doing more.
variations: Making it to today already makes you brave.
You don't have to feel better right away. Slow is okay too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by inner-critic self-judgment self-love theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你腦子裡那個一直罵你的聲音，
不是事實，只是習慣。
你可以練習，把它換成一句「沒關係，我在」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種老是自我攻擊、一犯錯就把自己貶到底的朋友。也適合你半夜又被腦內小劇場疲勞轟炸、需要有人幫你按暫停的時候。
variations: 自我檢討不等於自我懲罰。
那個聲音不是你，你可以選擇不聽它的。

en.content:
That voice in your head that keeps tearing you down
isn't the truth — it's just a habit.
You can practice swapping it for one line: 'It's okay, I've got you.'
en.editorNote: For the friend who constantly attacks themselves and spirals into self-loathing over one mistake. Also for those late nights when the mental replay won't stop and you need someone to hit pause for you.
variations: Self-reflection isn't the same as self-punishment.
That voice isn't you. You get to choose not to listen to it.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Anna Taylor 'love yourself enough to set boundaries' theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
設界線不是把人推開，
是告訴自己：我的時間和心力，值得被好好對待。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種老是不好意思拒絕別人、結果把自己累垮的朋友。也適合你又想脫口說「好啦沒關係」之前，先深呼吸一下的時候。
variations: 會拒絕，不是壞人，是懂得保護自己。
你怎麼對待自己，別人就會學著怎麼對待你。

en.content:
Setting a boundary isn't pushing people away.
It's telling yourself: my time and energy deserve to be treated well.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can never bring themselves to say no and ends up running on empty. Also for that moment right before another automatic 'it's fine, don't worry' slips out — take a breath first.
variations: Saying no doesn't make you the bad guy. It means you know how to protect yourself.
The way you treat yourself teaches everyone else how to treat you.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Paulo Coelho 'saying yes to others while saying no to yourself' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
每一次對別人說「好」，
記得看一眼，你是不是同時對自己說了「不」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合那種永遠把別人的需求排在前面、忘記問自己想不想的人。貼在待辦清單旁邊，答應事情前先看一眼剛剛好。
variations: 把自己排第一，不是自私，是你也是一個人。
你不是無限量供應的，該省著點給。

en.content:
Every time you say 'yes' to someone else,
take a look — are you quietly saying 'no' to yourself?
en.editorNote: For the person who always puts everyone else's needs first and forgets to ask what they actually want. Stick it next to your to-do list — a good glance before you agree to one more thing.
variations: Putting yourself first isn't selfish. You're a person too.
You're not an unlimited supply. Spend yourself a little more carefully.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Max Lucado 'you are valuable simply because you exist' theme; rephrased with fresh framing

zh-tw.content:
你的價值不用靠做了什麼來證明。
光是你這個人存在，就已經算數了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給那種一沒生產力就覺得自己沒用的朋友。也適合你躺平耍廢一整天、又開始有罪惡感的時候，提醒你休息不用先賺來。
variations: 你不是一台機器，不用靠產出來換取被愛。
什麼都沒做的你，一樣值得。

en.content:
Your worth doesn't need proving by what you get done.
You existing at all — that already counts.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels useless the moment they stop being productive. Also for the day you did nothing but lie around and the guilt starts creeping in — rest isn't something you have to earn first.
variations: You're not a machine. You don't have to earn love with output.
The you who did nothing today is worth just as much.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by Sharon Salzberg 'you deserve your own love and affection' theme; rephrased

zh-tw.content:
你花了那麼多力氣去愛別人、擔心別人，
偶爾也把那份溫柔，留一點給自己吧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合送給那種對誰都很好、就是對自己很兇的朋友。也適合你照顧完全世界、回到家發現自己空空的那個夜晚。
variations: 你也在你要照顧的名單上，別老是把自己漏掉。
對自己好一點，這件事你也有資格。

en.content:
You spend so much energy loving and worrying about everyone else —
now and then, save a little of that tenderness for yourself.
en.editorNote: For the friend who's gentle with everyone but brutal to themselves. Also for the night you've taken care of the whole world and come home to find yourself running on empty.
variations: You're on the list of people you're supposed to take care of too. Stop leaving yourself off it.
Being kind to yourself — you're allowed that one too.
